EvolveStep.step3: Collect user A/B decisions. Build confirmed evolution_plan. Pattern upgrade accepted→multi_file=true, include module list+node allocation. Accumulate {evolve_accepted_a, evolve_accepted_b, evolve_total_c}. CAP SCOPE(CR4): 'A+B: {count}/{cap} CAP | C: {total_c} (uncapped)'. cap=max(1,floor(TARGET_nodes*0.10)). EVOLVE_CAP_CORE_SEPARATION: TARGET_nodes for CAP calculation MUST exclude AIAP_Standard library modules. METHOD: (1) Enumerate all *.aisop.json files in target directory. (2) Partition into core_modules (files NOT matching 'AIAP_Standard.*.aisop.json' pattern) and standard_modules (files matching 'AIAP_Standard.*.aisop.json'). (3) core_node_count = sum of functional nodes across core_modules only. standard_node_count = sum of functional nodes across standard_modules. (4) TARGET_nodes = core_node_count (NOT total including standard). (5) Recalculate: structural_cap = max(1, floor(core_node_count * 0.10)). (6) Report: { total_nodes, core_nodes, standard_nodes, exclusion_pattern: 'AIAP_Standard.*.aisop.json', cap_before_separation: floor(total*0.10), cap_after_separation: floor(core*0.10) }. RATIONALE: AIAP_Standard modules are shared library components with independent versioning — they do not participate in the target program's evolution and should not inflate the evolution budget. This prevents over-permissive CAP values that allow too many simultaneous changes. CAP RATIONALE (P4 v3.8.0): small programs (N<20) yield CAP=1 by design — ensures each evolution cycle is focused and reviewable. Multi-feature evolution achievable through consecutive cycles. No other agent framework (LangGraph/CrewAI/AutoGen) has structural change budgets — AISOP CAP is unique. TARGET NODE RULE(C1): use post-evolution node count for CAP. Exceeds→ADVISORY: consider splitting cycles. EVOLVE CAP GRANULARITY: Distinguish B-changes by structural impact granularity to allow more internal refinements while maintaining structural change discipline. CLASSIFICATION: for each accepted B-change, classify as: (a) NODE-LEVEL (structural_change): change involves Mermaid graph modification — adding/removing/rewiring nodes, new sub_mermaid graphs, or pattern upgrade. Detection: change description mentions 'new node', 'add node to Mermaid', 'new function with Mermaid entry', or target is a previously non-existent function requiring new Mermaid routing. (b) STEP-LEVEL (internal_change): change adds/modifies steps within an EXISTING function without Mermaid graph changes — new step1_5, enhanced step3, additional sub-checks. Detection: change targets an existing function's step, does NOT require new Mermaid nodes. CAP RULES: structural_cap = max(1, floor(TARGET_nodes * 0.10)) (original formula, unchanged). ADAPTIVE COMPLEXITY FACTOR: complexity_factor = min(2.0, max(1.0, log2(TARGET_nodes) / 4)). internal_cap = ceil(structural_cap * complexity_factor). Examples: 10 nodes → factor=1.0 → internal=1, 40 nodes → factor=1.33 → internal=6, 171 nodes → factor=1.85 → internal=32, 500 nodes → factor=2.0(capped) → internal=100. RATIONALE: small programs (N<20) get factor≈1.0, constraining internal changes to match structural cap — prevents over-editing simple programs. Large programs (N>100) get factor approaching 2.0, allowing proportionally more internal refinements. Log2 ensures diminishing returns — doubling nodes doesn't double internal budget. TOTAL CONSTRAINT: structural_count ≤ structural_cap AND (structural_count + internal_count) ≤ structural_cap + internal_cap (total budget = sum, not 3× multiplier). DISPLAY: 'EVOLVE CAP: {structural_count}/{structural_cap} structural + {internal_count}/{internal_cap} internal = {total}/{total_cap} total changes.' RATIONALE: step-level changes (adding a sub-check within ThreeDimTest, enhancing a constraint verification) are analogous to method-body edits (EPR in research) — they don't alter control flow topology. Node-level changes (adding Mermaid nodes, new routing paths) are analogous to class-level refactorings (CGR) — they affect architectural structure. Treating them identically penalizes programs that need many quality refinements but no structural changes. BACKWARD COMPATIBLE: if classification cannot be determined (ambiguous change description), default to NODE-LEVEL (conservative). Previous behavior preserved for edge cases. DEPENDENCY MATRIX(PL18): map B-changes→affected_modules. Build cross-change graph: B_x.M_a produces field consumed by B_y.M_b→record edge. Output: {changes:[{id,modules}], dependencies:[{from,to,reason}], suggested_waves:[[modules]]}. AIAP_MD pseudo-module for metadata-only changes→Wave 1. All AIAP_MD only→metadata_only_mode=true. WAVE ISOLATION: parallel modules→namespaced wave_results.{module}, merged by orchestrator. Global fields written ONLY by orchestrator. Conflict→last-writer-wins+merge_log. Present as mermaid graph. Bidirectional→CIRCULAR_DEPENDENCY advisory. CASCADE(cascade_on_reject): rejected LEVEL_A→traverse deps→only-rejected-deps→auto-reject(cascade), mixed-deps→DEGRADED(use defaults). Present cascade summary, user override. Record cascade_results. DRY-RUN GATE: dry_run=true→present full plan(A/B/C,deps,nodes,files,waves)+preview report{projected_version,changes,affected_files,node_delta,risk_assessment}. User: confirm(proceed)→abort(terminate). CAP_ADVISORY (B4 v1.5.0 + B4 v1.6.0 DIMENSIONAL REPORTING — Kubernetes ResourceQuota per-dimension used/hard monitoring per resource type; kubectl describe quota dimensional output): After CAP computation, check utilization percentage PER DIMENSION and overall. DIMENSIONAL UTILIZATION: compute three utilization metrics independently: structural_pct = (structural_count * self_evo_multiplier) / structural_cap * 100, internal_pct = (internal_count * self_evo_multiplier) / internal_cap * 100, total_pct = (structural_count * self_evo_multiplier + internal_count * self_evo_multiplier) / (structural_cap + internal_cap) * 100. DIMENSIONAL THRESHOLDS: apply thresholds to EACH dimension independently — any single dimension ≥80% triggers ADVISORY even if total is below 80%. This prevents total utilization masking dimensional saturation (e.g., structural at 95% but total at 50% due to low internal usage). STRUCTURAL EMPHASIS: structural dimension approaching saturation is flagged with higher severity than internal, because structural changes have greater architectural impact. FORMAT: 'EVOLVE CAP Advisory: Structural: {s_used}/{s_cap} ({s_pct}%) — {s_level} | Internal: {i_used}/{i_cap} ({i_pct}%) — {i_level} | Total: {t_used}/{t_cap} ({t_pct}%) — {t_level}'. OVERALL THRESHOLDS: (a) max(structural_pct, internal_pct, total_pct) < 80% → advisory_level='NORMAL', no advisory. (b) any dimension 80%-99% → advisory_level='ADVISORY': emit dimensional breakdown with remaining capacity per dimension. (c) any dimension = 100% → advisory_level='WARNING': emit 'FULL' for saturated dimension. (d) any dimension > 100% → advisory_level='EXCEEDED': blocking — must reduce. Record cap_advisory = { structural_pct, internal_pct, total_pct, advisory_level: 'NORMAL'|'ADVISORY'|'WARNING'|'EXCEEDED', saturated_dimensions: [string] } in pipeline_metadata. CROSS_MODULE_IMPACT_PREDICTION: Before presenting B-changes to user for confirmation, build CROSS-MODULE DEPENDENCY GRAPH and predict impact classification for each proposed change. PURPOSE: when proposing B-changes targeting specific modules, no mechanism predicts cross-module impact — a change in SimulateStep constraints may require corresponding awareness in ReviewStep, but this dependency is discovered during execution rather than planning. METHOD: (1) DEPENDENCY GRAPH CONSTRUCTION: from pipeline_metadata.module_classification (populated by Research3), build module_dependency_graph: for each module, extract all references to other modules' output fields from function step text. Edge: module_A → module_B means A consumes data produced by B. (2) PER-CHANGE IMPACT ANALYSIS: for each proposed B-change in evolution_plan.changes: (a) identify target_module and target_function from change specification. (b) extract OUTPUT fields of the target function (patterns: 'output:', 'emit', 'record', 'store', 'set pipeline_metadata.{field}'). (c) scan all OTHER modules for references to these output fields. (d) build consumer_list = [{ module, function, field_referenced }]. (3) IMPACT CLASSIFICATION: (a) 'isolated' — consumer_list is empty (no cross-module consumers). No additional awareness needed. (b) 'propagating' — consumer_list has 1-2 entries. Advisory: these consumers may need to be aware of changes. (c) 'cascading' — consumer_list has 3+ entries OR consumer chain depth >= 2 (consumer of consumer). WARNING: 'Change B{id} to {module}.{function} has CASCADING impact — {N} downstream consumers across {M} modules may be affected: [{consumer_details}]. Review cross-module compatibility.' (4) USER PRESENTATION: in EvolveStep tier presentation, include impact_class for each B-change: '| B{id} | {feature} | {impact_class} | {consumer_count} consumers |'. Cascading changes highlighted. (5) OUTPUT: impact_prediction = [{ change_id: string, target_module: string, target_function: string, output_fields: [string], impact_class: 'isolated'|'propagating'|'cascading', consumer_count: int, consumers: [{ module: string, function: string, field: string }] }]. Log: 'IMPACT_PREDICTION: {changes} changes analyzed — {isolated} isolated, {propagating} propagating, {cascading} cascading.' SKIP: if module_classification unavailable (standalone mode), or Create mode (no existing cross-module dependencies). Pass evolution_plan→Generate1.
EvolveStep.step3_5: EVOLUTION PLAN PRE-FLIGHT VALIDATOR: After collecting user A/B decisions and building confirmed evolution_plan (step3), BEFORE fractal check (step4), execute pre-flight validation on the finalized plan to catch structural conflicts that would cause Generate1 failures. PURPOSE: prevent invalid evolution plans from reaching generation stage — fail fast with actionable diagnostics. METHOD: (1) STEP COLLISION DETECTION: for each pair of accepted B-changes, compare target coordinates {module, function, step_id}. If two B-changes target the SAME function AND the same step_id (e.g., both add step3_5 to ValidateStep) → RED 'PRE-FLIGHT-COLLISION: {B_x} and {B_y} both target {function}.{step_id}. Resolution: rename one step_id (e.g., step3_5 → step3_6) or merge changes.' If two B-changes target the same function but DIFFERENT steps → PASS (independent modifications). Report: collisions = [{ change_a, change_b, function, step_id }]. (2) DATA FLOW PRE-VALIDATION: for each B-change that declares new_output_fields in its specification, scan all OTHER accepted B-changes for consumption references to those fields. If B_x produces field_A and B_y consumes field_A but B_x was REJECTED → CASCADE_WARNING: '{B_y} depends on {field_A} produced by rejected {B_x}. Options: [Accept B_x to satisfy dependency] [Modify B_y to remove dependency] [Reject B_y (cascade)].' Build dependency_satisfaction_map: { field → { producer: change_id|null, consumers: [change_id], satisfied: boolean } }. Any unsatisfied → YELLOW. (3) CONSTRAINT COMPATIBILITY CHECK: for each B-change, extract the NEW constraints or logic it introduces. Compare against the target function's EXISTING constraints field. Detect contradictions: (a) new step performs 'write' but existing constraints declare 'Read-only' → CONSTRAINT_CONFLICT RED, (b) new step adds 'auto-apply' logic but existing constraints declare 'Never auto-approve' → CONSTRAINT_CONFLICT RED, (c) new step adds retry logic but existing Error field already has circuit-breaker at lower threshold → CONSTRAINT_OVERLAP YELLOW (not conflict, but redundancy). (4) PARAMETER AVAILABILITY CHECK: for each B-change that references parameters in its logic, verify those parameters exist in the target module's params{} section OR are produced by a prior step in the same function. Missing parameter → YELLOW 'PRE-FLIGHT-PARAM-MISSING: {B_change} references {param} but target module does not declare it.' (5) UNIFIED IMPACT PRE-SCAN: Merge pre-flight structural validation with forward-looking impact scope scanning into a SINGLE PASS over the evolution_plan. PURPOSE: eliminate redundant traversal — pre-flight (step3_5) and impact scope (step5_5) both iterate over all B-changes and their target modules. By co-locating these analyses, detect conflicts AND impacts simultaneously, enabling earlier risk visibility. METHOD: (a) During collision detection (check 1), SIMULTANEOUSLY build the change_registry needed by step5_5 impact scope analysis: extract { change_id, target_module, target_function, target_step, new_output_fields, modified_thresholds, new_tool_calls } for each B-change. (b) During data flow pre-validation (check 2), SIMULTANEOUSLY perform MF14 consumer tracing: for each new_output_field, trace forward through cross-module consumers using loaded module Mermaid graphs. Build preliminary impact_chains. (c) During constraint compatibility check (check 3), SIMULTANEOUSLY check for cross-module constraint propagation gaps: if new constraint in module A affects data consumed by module B, verify B can accommodate. (d) OUTPUT ENRICHMENT: extend pre_flight_report with unified fields: pre_flight_report.impact_preview = { change_registry: [ChangeEntry], preliminary_impact_chains: [ChainEntry], cross_module_constraint_flags: [FlagEntry], estimated_risk_level: 'low'|'medium'|'high' }. estimated_risk_level uses same formula as step5_5 risk_score but with preliminary data (may be refined by step5_5 full analysis). (e) STEP5_5 OPTIMIZATION: when step5_5 executes, it SHOULD check for pre_flight_report.impact_preview in pipeline_context. If present, skip redundant change_registry extraction and MF14 consumer tracing — use pre-computed data. Only perform step5_5-specific analyses (threshold ripple deep scan, tool scope gap verification, risk quantification refinement). Log: 'UNIFIED VALIDATOR: impact_preview consumed from pre-flight — {N} change entries reused, {M} impact chains carried forward.' (f) CROSS-VALIDATION: after step5_5 completes its refined analysis, compare preliminary estimated_risk_level from pre-flight with final risk_level from step5_5. If they DISAGREE (e.g., pre-flight='low' but step5_5='high') → emit INFO 'RISK-ESTIMATE-DRIFT: pre-flight estimated {est} but full analysis found {actual}. Delta likely due to {reason: threshold_ripples_discovered|transitive_consumers_found}.' This helps calibrate future pre-flight estimates. (6) OUTPUT: pre_flight_report = { collisions: [CollisionEntry], cascade_warnings: [CascadeEntry], constraint_conflicts: [ConflictEntry], constraint_overlaps: [OverlapEntry], param_missing: [ParamEntry], impact_preview: { change_registry, preliminary_impact_chains, cross_module_constraint_flags, estimated_risk_level }, pass: boolean }. pass = true ONLY if zero RED items. (7) GATE: if pass=false → present pre_flight_report to user, return to step2 for plan adjustment. If pass=true → proceed to step4 fractal check. Max 2 re-adjustments then FORCE_PASS with warnings. SKIP: Create mode (no evolution_plan), or evolution_plan contains only LEVEL_C changes (no B-change interactions to validate).
EvolveStep.step4: FRACTAL CHECK per STANDARD.C5 + AIAP_Protocol.md: Calculate projected functional nodes PER FILE (for Pattern B+, each sub-module checked independently). NON-UPGRADE: total = current + accepted additions per file. PATTERN UPGRADE (e.g., A→B): calculate PER-FILE projected nodes after proposed split — each sub-module must satisfy 3-12 (Standard) or apply AIAP_Protocol.md progressive thresholds. Apply convergence node rules from AIAP_Protocol.md. Functional nodes per STANDARD.C5.functional_node_count (total Mermaid nodes - Start - endNode). CHECK 1 — PATTERN: if 2+ independent functions detected (per independence test) and currently Pattern A -> suggest LEVEL_A: Pattern A->B upgrade (main as router + func modules). CHECK 2 — PROGRESSIVE: projected functional nodes 13-15 -> ADVISORY: 'Module has {N} functional nodes. Review extraction opportunities.' Analyze and suggest splits by tool boundary > data flow phase > functional independence. Projected functional nodes 16+ -> RECOMMENDED: 'Module has {N} functional nodes. Strongly recommend splitting.' Provide concrete split plan as LEVEL_A suggestions. CHECK 3 — SHARED: if 2+ modules share complex logic -> suggest Pattern C. All fractal suggestions presented as LEVEL_A items for user decision.
EvolveStep.step5: MIGRATION IMPACT ANALYSIS: For each proposed new feature, analyze: (a) What data does it require? (b) Do existing users already have this data? (c) If not, what migration or bootstrap path is needed? Include migration strategy as part of the evolution proposal. Example: 'New feature PersonalityInsight requires personality_archetype field → existing profiles lack this → LoadProfile must add schema migration v1.0→v2.0 with default null.' This prevents migration being an afterthought. VERSION OVERFLOW ADVISORY (per P3.5): after computing version bump per STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management, check: if patch >= 9 → emit ADVISORY: 'Patch version {version} approaching overflow (x.y.9+). Consider consolidating recent patches into a minor version bump for the next evolution.' if minor >= 19 → emit ADVISORY: 'Minor version {version} is high. Consider a major version bump if significant architectural changes are planned.' Advisory only — does not change version computation.
EvolveStep.constraints: MODE ROUTING: step1 handles Create mode (scope confirmation per CR6). step2-step5 handle Evolve mode (tier presentation, user decisions, dependency matrix, fractal check). Both modes converge at Generate1 delegation. LEVEL_A items MUST have user confirmation — never auto-apply. Max 2 re-research cycles on rejection. Fractal suggestions are LEVEL_A items — require user confirmation. Never force split — user decides. PATTERN_UPGRADE_IMPACT: if accepted LEVEL_A includes Pattern upgrade, present impact summary (projected file count, node distribution per module, tools redistribution) for user confirmation before proceeding. MODULE BOUNDARY CHECK: Before proposing a change that: (a) adds tools to a zero-tool module, (b) changes idempotent from true to false, (c) adds side_effects where there were none, or (d) increases trust_level — generate a BOUNDARY CHANGE NOTIFICATION: 'WARNING: Proposed change modifies {module}.aisop.json boundaries: {changes}. Trust level implications: {old_trust}→{new_trust}. Require explicit user approval before proceeding.' Mark as LEVEL_C change (requires user confirmation) regardless of original evolution level.
EvolveStep.step5_5: EVOLUTION IMPACT SCOPE ANALYSIS: After migration impact analysis (step5) and before attestation, perform CROSS-MODULE IMPACT TRACING for each accepted LEVEL_B change. PURPOSE: identify indirect dependencies and downstream consumers that may be affected by the evolution but are NOT explicitly listed in evolution_plan — preventing drift from untracked side effects. METHOD: (1) CHANGE REGISTRY: for each accepted B-change, extract { change_id, target_module, target_function, target_step, new_output_fields: [string], modified_thresholds: [{ field, old, new }], new_tool_calls: [string] }. (2) MF14 CONSUMER TRACE: using MF14 cross-module field consumer validation data (from EvolveStep.step3 dependency_matrix), trace each new_output_field forward through the consumer graph. For each field: find all modules that consume it (directly or transitively via intermediary modules). Build impact_chain: [{ field, producer: change_id, consumers: [{ module, function, step, consumption_type: 'direct'|'transitive' }] }]. (3) THRESHOLD RIPPLE: for each modified_threshold, scan ALL modules for steps that reference the same threshold name or derived metrics. If threshold 'engagement_score >= 0.6' is changed to '0.4', find all modules that use 'engagement_score' in conditions. Flag: 'THRESHOLD-RIPPLE: {change_id} modifies {threshold} in {module}. Also referenced by: [{other_modules}]. Verify downstream logic remains consistent.' (4) TOOL SCOPE CHANGE: for each new_tool_call, verify the target module's AIAP.md tool declaration includes the tool. If a B-change introduces a tool call to a module that doesn't declare that tool → RED: 'TOOL-SCOPE-GAP: {change_id} adds {tool} call to {module} but module tools[] does not include {tool}.' (5) IMPACT REPORT WITH STANDARDIZED SCHEMA: impact_scope_report follows STANDARD.data_contracts.impact_scope_report json_schema: { changes_analyzed: int, direct_impacts: [ImpactEntry { change_id: string, module: string, function: string, impact_type: 'direct_modification' }], indirect_impacts: [ImpactEntry { change_id: string, consumer_module: string, consumer_function: string, impact_type: 'field_consumption'|'threshold_reference'|'tool_dependency', severity: 'high'|'medium'|'low' }], untracked_consumers: [{ field: string, expected_consumer: string, status: 'not_in_evolution_plan' }], threshold_ripples: [RippleEntry { threshold: string, source_change: string, affected_modules: [string], cumulative_delta: float|null }], tool_scope_gaps: [GapEntry { change_id: string, tool: string, module: string }], risk_score: float(0.0-1.0), risk_level: 'low'|'medium'|'high' }. RISK QUANTIFICATION: risk_score = min(1.0, (indirect_impacts.length * 0.15 + threshold_ripples.length * 0.25 + tool_scope_gaps.length * 0.40 + untracked_consumers.filter(severity='high').length * 0.20) / normalization_factor). normalization_factor = max(1, changes_analyzed). risk_level: risk_score < 0.3 → 'low', risk_score < 0.7 → 'medium', risk_score >= 0.7 → 'high'. (6) ADVISORY OUTPUT: present impact_scope_report to user: 'IMPACT SCOPE: {N} changes analyzed, {M} direct impacts, {K} indirect impacts, {T} threshold ripples. Risk: {risk_level} ({risk_score:.2f}).' risk_level='high' → YELLOW advisory with mandatory review recommendation. Tool scope gaps → RED mandatory fix. (7) GENERATE1 PRIORITY ROUTING: pass impact_scope_report to Generate1. Generate1 SHOULD prioritize high-risk changes for generation and ThreeDimTest: changes with indirect_impacts.length > 2 OR associated threshold_ripples are generated first and tested first, enabling early detection of cross-module issues. (8) HISTORY PERSISTENCE: append impact_scope_report summary to quality_baseline.json evolution_history entry for this version: { risk_score, risk_level, indirect_impact_count, threshold_ripple_count }. This enables cross-version impact trend analysis — if risk_score is consistently increasing across evolutions, flag IMPACT_ESCALATION_TREND. (9) CROSS-MODULE DATA FLOW AUTO-DOCUMENTATION: After impact scope analysis, automatically extract and document cross-module data flows from evolution changes. PURPOSE: ensure MF14 cross-module field validation has complete, up-to-date flow declarations — preventing silent field consumption mismatches that impact analysis alone cannot detect (impact analysis traces known fields; data flow documentation discovers undeclared flows). METHOD: (a) FLOW EXTRACTION WITH TIERED PATTERN LIBRARY: scan each accepted B-change's step text using 3-tier pattern matching: L1 PRECISE PATTERNS (high confidence): 'pass X to Y', 'output X to Y', 'consume X from Y', 'delegate to Y — pass X', 'Y reads X from', 'forward X to'. L2 EXTENDED PATTERNS (medium confidence, new): 'send X to Y', 'inject X into Y', 'provide X for Y', 'deliver X to Y', 'emit X to Y', 'return X to Y', 'receive X from Y', 'load X from Y', 'write X to Y', 'attach X to Y', 'propagate X to Y', 'feed X into Y'. L3 CONTEXTUAL PATTERNS (low confidence, new): any '{module_A}.{function} → {module_B}' cross-module reference pattern + any step text containing 'to {ModuleName}' or 'from {ModuleName}' adjacent to a field name (within 5 words). MATCH PRIORITY: L1 > L2 > L3. L3 matches are tagged confidence='low' and displayed separately in documentation. DEDUP: if same flow matched by multiple tiers, use highest-confidence tier only. For each match, extract: { field: string, producer_module: string, producer_function: string, consumer_module: string, consumer_function: string, flow_type: 'parameter'|'file_artifact'|'delegation_context' }. (b) DATA FLOW MAP CONSTRUCTION: merge extracted flows with existing data_flow declarations in target program's AIAP.md or module headers. Build data_flow_map: { total_flows: int, new_flows: [FlowEntry], existing_flows_modified: [FlowEntry], existing_flows_unchanged: [FlowEntry] }. DEDUP: if extracted flow matches an existing declaration (same field+producer+consumer), classify as 'unchanged' unless flow_type changed. (c) CONFLICT DETECTION: for each new_flow, check if field name collides with an existing flow from a different producer → YELLOW: 'DATA-FLOW-CONFLICT: field {field} produced by both {producer_A} and {producer_B}. Resolve: rename, namespace, or merge.' (d) DOCUMENTATION GENERATION: auto-generate data_flow_documentation segment: { version: string, generated_at: ISO8601, flows_documented: int, new_flows: int, modified_flows: int, flow_entries: [{ field, producer_module, producer_function, consumer_module, consumer_function, flow_type, source_change_id: string|null }] }. Attach to evolution_plan as evolution_plan.data_flow_documentation. (e) MF14 INTEGRATION: pass data_flow_documentation to Generate1 ThreeDimTest. Generate1 MF14 cross-module field validation SHOULD use data_flow_documentation.flow_entries as ground truth for field producer/consumer verification — any field consumed but not in flow_entries → 'UNDOCUMENTED-FLOW' warning. (f) OUTPUT: data_flow_doc = { flows_documented: int, new_flows: int, existing_flows_modified: int, conflicts: int }. Log: 'DATA FLOW DOC: {flows_documented} flows documented ({new_flows} new, {existing_flows_modified} modified, {conflicts} conflicts).' SKIP: Create mode (no cross-module impact), or single-module programs, or evolution_plan contains only LEVEL_C changes.
EvolveStep.step6_attestation: ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'EvolveStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=4, stage_name='EvolveStep', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
EvolveStep.Error: User rejects all -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: proceed with LEVEL_C only -> inform. CREATE MODE SCOPE CONFIRM (CR6): scope confirmation form display fails or user rejects all modules -> retry -> fallback: auto-confirm with Research1 scope_recommendation defaults (if available) or single-module Pattern A minimal design -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

Generate1.step1: [ASSERT] EvolveStep executed. If false → go back to EvolveStep. | Delegate to generate.aisop.json — pass plan (from Research1/EvolveStep), evolution_plan (from EvolveStep), evolution_context (old_version_context from pipeline context for evolution verification), workspace_dir, quality_threshold. WAVE-BASED GENERATION (P2b): if EvolveStep.dependency_matrix.suggested_waves exists AND total files > 6, activate wave mode: iterate through suggested_waves array, for each wave call generate.aisop.json with wave_context = { wave_index, wave_files, total_waves }. Between waves, run quick ThreeDimTest on completed wave files (tier=Simple, C+D dimensions only). If any wave scores < 3.6, pause for review before next wave. PROGRESS: update pipeline_task.progress with sub-wave granularity (e.g. 'Generate Wave 2/3'). If suggested_waves not present or total files ≤ 6, use standard single-pass generation (backward compatible).
Generate1.step2: Collect draft_path, test_report, traffic_report. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { generate1_version }. IMPACT_SCAN (B1 v1.4.0 + B2 v1.6.0 DYNAMIC READ — Bazel BUILD explicit dependency declaration with Bzlmod MVS resolution; Gradle dynamic dependency consumption strategy; Policy-as-Code metadata dependency validation): After generation completes, scan all modified fields for dependent metadata that requires synchronization. DYNAMIC DEPENDENCY RESOLUTION: PREFER reading structural_impact_matrix from pipeline_metadata (produced by Research1 StructureAnalysis B2 v1.5.0). If pipeline_metadata.structural_impact_matrix exists AND structural_impact_matrix.dependencies is non-empty: (1) For each modified field, look up matching 'from' entries in structural_impact_matrix.dependencies. (2) For each matched dependency, use sync_type to determine action: 'version' → flag all 'to' targets for version sync, 'count' → recount and compare 'to' targets, 'description' → check 'to' targets mention the change, 'delegation' → verify call parameters match COLLECT list, 'data_flow' → verify consumer field schemas. (3) Set impact_source='structural_impact_matrix'. If pipeline_metadata.structural_impact_matrix NOT available (legacy pipeline or Research1 skipped): FALLBACK to hardcoded DEPENDENCY RULES: (a) fractal_exempt modified → check summary/description node counts match, check AIAP.md total_nodes. (b) sub_mermaid added/removed → check fractal_exempt sub-graph count and node breakdown. (c) params added/removed → check description mentions new parameter. (d) functions added/removed → check fractal_exempt functional node count. (e) version changed → flag all files requiring version sync. Set impact_source='hardcoded_rules'. OUTPUT: impact_checklist = [{ field_modified: string, dependent_fields: [string], sync_needed: boolean, auto_fixable: boolean }]. If any sync_needed=true AND auto_fixable=true → apply auto-fix immediately. If sync_needed=true AND auto_fixable=false → emit YELLOW: 'IMPACT_SCAN: {field_modified} changed but {dependent_field} requires manual update.' Record pipeline_metadata.impact_scan_result = { scanned: int, auto_fixed: int, manual_required: int, impact_source: 'structural_impact_matrix'|'hardcoded_rules' }. Log: 'IMPACT_SCAN: source={impact_source}, {scanned} fields scanned, {auto_fixed} auto-fixed, {manual_required} manual.' PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='Generate1', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'Generate1', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass draft_path and test_report to Research2. INCREMENTAL: if module_hashes available from PipelineStart, pass to generate for selective regeneration of changed modules only. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=5, stage_name='Generate1', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
Generate1.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. This is the first generation pass — produces initial draft. DATA FLOW CONTRACT REFERENCE: Key pipeline parameters and their flow: (a) research_context — ALWAYS named per STANDARD.PL7, carries mode-specific research results (structure/quality/compliance). (b) evolution_context — old_version_context wrapper for comparative analysis (null in Create mode). (c) evolution_summary — current evolution change summary { changed_modules, new_features, level_a/b_changes } passed to Research2 for quality focus. (d) evolution_plan — confirmed LEVEL_A/B/C changes with dependency_matrix from EvolveStep. (e) research_quality_context — separate quality-mode research results passed to SimulateStep for domain scenario enrichment (distinct from compliance-mode research_context). CROSS-MODULE ROUTING CHECK: After generating or modifying a sub-module's operation routing (e.g., adding new operations to OperationGate), verify that main.aisop.json has routing paths to invoke each new operation. Check: (1) Does NLU have intent keywords that could route to the new operation? (2) Does a handler node (e.g., DataPrivacy, ProfileQuery) have a sub-route to the new operation? If no routing path exists, emit WARN: 'New operation {op_name} in {module}.aisop.json has no entry point from main.NLU. Suggested fix: add {keywords} to NLU.step1(section) and/or add sub-route to {handler_node}.step1.' This check runs after each sub-module generation, not just at Validate. TOOL ANNOTATION FORMAT: When generating or modifying a module's tools section, ALWAYS use the structured annotation format: [{name: string, required: boolean, annotations: {read_only: boolean, destructive: boolean, idempotent: boolean, open_world: boolean}}]. Plain string format (e.g., ['file_system']) is NOT permitted. If existing modules in the target program use structured format, new/modified modules MUST match. Validate format consistency across all modules at Finalize.
Generate1.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display AI-generated skeleton -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

Research2.step1: [ASSERT] Generate1 executed. If false → go back to Generate1. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify draft_path from Generate1 is non-null and file exists via file_system. If null or missing -> fallback: (a) Evolve mode: use target_path from old_version_context. (b) Create mode (old_version_context is null): use draft_path directory from Generate1 output, log WARNING in pipeline_metadata: 'Research2 input null in Create mode — using Generate1 output directory.' CACHE CHECK (per STANDARD.PL17): compute cache_key = SHA-256(target_content_hash + 'quality' + workspace_dir). Look up .version_history/.cache/{cache_key}.json. If found AND age < TTL (from pipeline_context.cache_policy or base TTL) AND cache_bypass=false: load cached research_context, record cache_hit in pipeline_metadata.cache_hits, skip delegation. Else: record 'Research2' in pipeline_metadata.cache_misses. Delegate to research.aisop.json — pass topic (original user topic from pipeline context), workspace_dir, research_mode='quality', evolution_summary (from pipeline_context.evolution_plan: { changed_modules: [string], new_features: [string], level_a_changes: [string], level_b_changes: [string] }). evolution_summary is null for Create mode or when evolution_plan is unavailable. On success: write result to cache.
Research2.step2: Collect research_context (with research_mode='quality'). Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { research2_verified: research_context.verified_count }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='Research2', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'Research2', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass research_context to ModifyStep. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=6, stage_name='Research2', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
Research2.constraints: ALWAYS delegate (unless cache hit). Focuses on implementation quality: error handling, data validation, UX patterns, performance, domain best practices. Excludes regulatory compliance and formal threat assessment (those belong to Research3). ASSERT_CHAIN_CHECK: For Evolve mode, after research delegation returns, additionally verify [ASSERT] chain integrity for all new/modified nodes: (a) parse each node's step1 [ASSERT] condition, (b) verify referenced predecessor node exists in Mermaid graph, (c) verify reference direction matches Mermaid edge direction, (d) for sub_mermaid called nodes: [ASSERT] should reference 'Called via run aisop.{sub_mermaid_name}' pattern instead of predecessor node name. Broken chain → append to research_context.quality_findings as YELLOW: 'ASSERT_CHAIN: node {name} references non-existent predecessor {ref}.' Include assert_chain_result = { checked: int, valid: int, broken: [string] } in research_context.
Research2.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: proceed without research_context -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ModifyStep.step1: [ASSERT] Research2∨QualityGate∨PostSimulateGate executed. If false → go back to Research2. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify research_context from Research2 is non-null and contains verified_findings array. If null → trigger fallback: proceed without research_context, set research_context={verified_findings:[], verified_count:0, source:'fallback'} and log warning in pipeline_metadata. Delegate to modify.aisop.json — pass target_file (draft_path from Generate1), research_context (from Research2), evolution_context (from pipeline context for evolution-aware suggestion weighting), workspace_dir, user_request (original user request from pipeline context for modification intent), vision_context (from pipeline context, null if not Create mode). EVOLUTION_CONTEXT CONSUMPTION: when passing evolution_context to modify.aisop.json, include priority_directive: 'Prioritize quality fixes for LEVEL_B new functionality (highest regression risk from new code), then LEVEL_C modified areas, then existing unchanged code (lowest priority). evolution_context.change_ids identifies which changes are B vs C level.' This ensures the Modifier focuses on newly-introduced code quality issues first.
ModifyStep.step2: Collect modified_file_path, changes_applied count, and net_token_impact. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { modify_fixes: changes_applied, modify_net_token_impact: net_token_impact }. RESEARCH2 RECONCILIATION (CR7): After ModifyStep completes all fixes, iterate through research_context.quality_findings (from Research2). For each finding that was addressed by a ModifyStep fix, update finding status: { original_severity: RED|YELLOW, status: 'fixed_in_modifystep', fix_description: string, fix_step: string }. Store as pipeline_metadata.research2_reconciled_findings. Pass to Generate2 (ThreeDimTest) so it can perform verification re-test on fixed items rather than independent re-discovery. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='ModifyStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'ModifyStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass modified_file_path to Generate2. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=7, stage_name='ModifyStep', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
ModifyStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Modify applies quality improvements based on Research2 findings. CALL_CHAIN_VERIFY: After ModifyStep applies fixes, verify sub_mermaid call chain consistency: (a) find all 'run aisop.{sub_mermaid_name}' invocations across modified steps, (b) for each invocation, extract passed parameters from the calling step text, (c) compare against target sub_mermaid entry node's step1 COLLECT parameter list, (d) missing parameter in caller → auto-append to caller's invocation text with placeholder '{PARAM_NAME}' (NOT a default value — forces explicit resolution during generation), (e) extra parameter in caller (not in COLLECT list) → YELLOW advisory. Record call_chain_verify = { invocations_checked: int, params_auto_fixed: int, mismatches: [{ caller, callee, param, action }] } in pipeline_metadata.
ModifyStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip quality improvement -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

Generate2.step1: [ASSERT] ModifyStep executed. If false → go back to ModifyStep. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify modified_file_path from ModifyStep is non-null and file exists. If null → fallback: use draft_path from Generate1 and log warning in pipeline_metadata. Delegate to generate.aisop.json — pass modified_file_path, workspace_dir, quality_threshold.
Generate2.step2: Collect draft_path, test_report, traffic_report, weighted_score. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { generate2_weighted_score: weighted_score }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='Generate2', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'Generate2', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass test_report and weighted_score to QualityGate. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=8, stage_name='Generate2', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
Generate2.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. This is the quality retest pass — runs ThreeDimTest on modified draft to verify quality improvements. NOTE: ThreeDimTest now actively generates sub_mermaid decomposition proposals (C5-SUB_MERMAID-DECOMPOSE) when node count >= 16 and feasible sub-graphs are detected. If Generate2 produces a RED_WITH_FIX result with sub_mermaid_proposal, the pipeline routes through PauseHuman for user approval before ApplyFixes.step1_5 executes the decomposition.
Generate2.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use Generate1 draft with warning -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

QualityGate.step1: [ASSERT] Generate2 executed. If false → go back to Generate2. | Evaluate weighted_score from test_report (C*0.25 + I*0.45 + D*0.30). Threshold: strict>=3.6, standard>=2.7, relaxed>=2.0.
QualityGate.step2: PASS (weighted_score >= threshold) -> Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { quality_gate_result: 'PASS', quality_gate_retries: retry_count }. EARLY_BASELINE_CREATE: On PASS, if quality_baseline.json does not exist OR quality_baseline.json has no evolution_history array: write current ThreeDimTest results as initial_baseline to quality_baseline.json with { baseline_source: 'auto_created_in_quality_gate', version: current_version, weighted_score, grade, C/I/D scores, snapshot_date: ISO_now }. This enables QRG1 regression detection for subsequent pipeline stages (PostSimulateGate) and future evolutions. Does NOT conflict with ReviewStep step4 auto-scaffolding — ReviewStep overwrites with final scores, QualityGate provides mid-pipeline reference. Log: 'EARLY_BASELINE: quality_baseline.json created at QualityGate (score: {score}/{grade}).' ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'QualityGate', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. MID-PIPELINE CHAIN CHECK: invoke attestation_chain_validator at this checkpoint. Proceed to Research3. EVENT EMISSION (per STANDARD.PL20 event_hooks): if pipeline_context.event_hooks configured, emit on_gate_pass event: { gate: 'QualityGate', result: 'PASS', weighted_score, timestamp }. TRUST LEVEL CHECK: if pipeline_task.effective_trust_level < AIAP.md trust_level.level, enforce minimum 'standard' quality_threshold regardless of configured threshold. Log trust downgrade enforcement. AUTO-DOWNGRADE TRIGGER: if test_report shows I2 security dimension score < dimension_floor (indicating security failure), automatically downgrade pipeline_task.effective_trust_level by 1 (minimum T1). Record { event: 'auto_downgrade', trigger: 'I2_security_floor_violation', old_level, new_level, stage: 'QualityGate' } in pipeline_metadata.trust_downgrade_events. This ensures security failures automatically restrict subsequent pipeline stages. FUNCTIONAL_NODE_COUNT_RULE: DEFINITION: functional_node_count = count of keys in functions{} object WHERE key != 'endNode'. This rule is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for all node counting across the pipeline. ALL consumers MUST use this identical rule: (a) MODULE_LEVEL_RECHECK in QualityGate.step2 — uses FUNCTIONAL_NODE_COUNT_RULE for AIAP.md cross-check, (b) MODULE_CLASSIFICATION_PERSIST in Research3.step2 — uses FUNCTIONAL_NODE_COUNT_RULE for node count enrichment (per B9 v1.6.0), (c) MF31d in ValidateStep.step3 — uses FUNCTIONAL_NODE_COUNT_RULE for fractal_exempt validation, (d) EvolveStep.step3 — uses FUNCTIONAL_NODE_COUNT_RULE for CAP TARGET_nodes calculation. Any deviation from this counting rule across consumers indicates a CONTRACT VIOLATION and should be flagged as RED: 'NODE_COUNT_CONTRACT: {consumer} uses non-standard counting method.' MODULE_LEVEL_RECHECK: After QualityGate PASS, perform a lightweight re-verification of module-level metadata consistency to catch drift introduced during Generate1/Generate2 code generation. PURPOSE: Generate1 structural changes and Generate2 ThreeDimTest may alter module node counts, descriptions, or side_effects without updating AIAP.md metadata — catching this at QualityGate (before Research3/Validate) prevents cascading metadata inconsistency. METHOD: (1) MODULE METADATA SNAPSHOT: for each .aisop.json file in target directory, read current functional node count (per FUNCTIONAL_NODE_COUNT_RULE defined above), module description (from top-level description field), and tools array. (2) AIAP.md CROSS-CHECK: read AIAP.md modules section. For each module entry, compare: (a) declared node count vs actual functional node count — mismatch → flag as RECHECK_DRIFT with { module, field: 'nodes', declared, actual }. (b) declared side_effects vs tools array implications (file_system write → side_effects should be true) — mismatch → flag. (3) SEVERITY: RECHECK_DRIFT items are ADVISORY only (do not trigger FAIL) — they are collected in pipeline_metadata.module_recheck_drift = [drift_items] for ReviewStep step7 MODULE METADATA SYNC to auto-correct. (4) OPTIMIZATION: if pipeline_metadata.evolution_plan exists, only recheck modules listed in evolution_plan.changes[].target_module (skip unchanged modules). If no evolution_plan → recheck all modules. (5) Log: 'MODULE_LEVEL_RECHECK: {checked} modules checked, {drift_count} drift items flagged for ReviewStep auto-correction.'
QualityGate.step3: FAIL -> classify failure_category from test_report: identify lowest-scoring dimension (cognitive/intrinsic/detail). Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { quality_gate_result: 'FAIL', quality_gate_failure_category: lowest_dim }. Route to ModifyStep with failure_category hint for targeted dimension-specific fixes. Track retry_count. EVENT EMISSION: if event_hooks configured, emit on_gate_fail event: { gate: 'QualityGate', result: 'FAIL', failure_category: lowest_dim, weighted_score, retry_count, timestamp }.
QualityGate.step4: DIMENSION FLOOR: if any individual dimension score (C, I, or D) falls below dimension_floor (strict: 3/B, standard: 2/C, relaxed: 1/D), classify as FAIL regardless of weighted_score — this prevents high scores in one dimension from masking critical failures in another. Priority: Intrinsic floor violations take precedence (I2 security is non-negotiable). When FAIL due to dimension_floor, set failure_category to the floored dimension and route to ModifyStep with targeted fix directive.
QualityGate.step5: GLOBAL RETRY BUDGET: init 3 in PipelineStart. Each ModifyStep passage(any gate)→decrement. 0→only FORCE_PASS(+quality_warning) or ABORT. Track global_retry_count. QRG1 REGRESSION(Evolve/Modify): delta=weighted_score-old_weighted_score. <-0.1→WARNING, <-0.3→ERROR+ModifyStep regression-fix. >=-0.1→log no regression. DENOMINATOR NORMALIZATION: if applicable items differ old→new (I/C/D dimensions), normalize old scores before delta comparison: (I) new_applicable_I_items≠old→recompute old I_avg with new items scored at existing mean, (C) node_count changed→normalize C5 fractal with new denominator, (D) module_count changed→normalize MF cross-module with new denominator. Use all 3 normalized dimensions for normalized_old_weighted_score. Log {dimension_changed, old/new_denominator, normalized_score}. Skip Create mode.
QualityGate.step5_5: DIMENSION_DELTA_ANALYSIS: When weighted_score delta < 0 (score decreased from previous version), automatically diagnose which dimension(s) caused the decline and classify the root cause. PURPOSE: enable informed rollback/continue decisions by providing actionable diagnosis instead of bare delta numbers. METHOD: (1) DIMENSION DELTAS: compute per-dimension delta: delta_C = current_C - baseline_C, delta_I = current_I - baseline_I, delta_D = current_D - baseline_D. Compute weighted contribution of each delta: contrib_C = delta_C * 0.25, contrib_I = delta_I * 0.45, contrib_D = delta_D * 0.30. (2) PRIMARY CONTRIBUTOR: identify dimension with largest negative weighted contribution as primary_contributor. If all deltas >= 0 but weighted delta < 0 due to rounding → classify as ROUNDING_ARTIFACT. (3) ROOT CAUSE CLASSIFICATION: (a) COMPLEXITY_GROWTH — primary_contributor is C AND (node_count increased OR new modules added OR new sub_mermaid added). Evidence: 'C dimension dropped due to increased structural complexity.' (b) SCORING_METHODOLOGY_CHANGE — primary_contributor's applicable_count changed between versions (e.g., I items N/A→applicable or vice versa). Evidence: 'I dimension score changed due to applicable item count shift ({old_count}→{new_count}).' (c) GENUINE_REGRESSION — primary_contributor dropped AND no structural/methodology change detected. Evidence: 'D dimension dropped on {check_id} — indicates potential functional regression.' (d) THRESHOLD_SHIFT — scoring thresholds or weights changed between versions. Evidence: 'Score change caused by threshold/weight configuration change, not content change.' (4) OUTPUT: dimension_delta_report = { overall_delta: float, primary_contributor: 'C'|'I'|'D'|'ROUNDING', root_cause_type: 'COMPLEXITY_GROWTH'|'SCORING_METHODOLOGY_CHANGE'|'GENUINE_REGRESSION'|'THRESHOLD_SHIFT'|'ROUNDING_ARTIFACT', per_dimension: { C: { prev: float, curr: float, delta: float, weighted_contrib: float }, I: { prev: float, curr: float, delta: float, weighted_contrib: float }, D: { prev: float, curr: float, delta: float, weighted_contrib: float } }, evidence: string }. (5) INTEGRATION: pass dimension_delta_report to ReviewStep Rollback Decision Engine as additional input. GENUINE_REGRESSION with delta < -0.15 → adds +1 to rollback high_count. COMPLEXITY_GROWTH and SCORING_METHODOLOGY_CHANGE → informational only, do not trigger rollback. (6) DISPLAY: when delta < 0, present: 'QUALITY DELTA ANALYSIS: {overall_delta:+.3f} — Primary: {primary_contributor} ({root_cause_type}). {evidence}.' SKIP: Create mode (no baseline), or delta >= 0 (no decline to diagnose).
QualityGate.step6: QRG3 ABSOLUTE FLOOR CHECK (ALL pipeline operations including Create): if weighted_score < 3.0, classify as FAIL regardless of threshold. Record pipeline_metadata.qrg3_triggered = true. Emit ERROR: 'QRG3 absolute quality floor breached (weighted_score < 3.0).' This check has no mode exemption — applies to Create, Evolve, and Modify pipelines per STANDARD.performance.quality_regression.QRG3. QRG-CREATE (CR12 v1.2.0, Create mode only): if old_version_context is null (Create mode), apply CREATE-specific quality gates as substitute for QRG1 regression check: (1) INITIAL QUALITY FLOOR: weighted_score >= 4.0 (Grade A minimum). If below: FAIL with 'QRG-CREATE: New program must achieve Grade A or higher (weighted_score >= 4.0). Current: {score}.' (2) SECURITY FLOOR: I2 score >= 4. If below: FAIL with 'QRG-CREATE: New program must have robust security guards (I2 >= 4). Current: {I2_score}.' (3) MODULE BALANCE: for Pattern B+ programs, compute max_module_nodes / min_module_nodes ratio. If ratio > 3.0: YELLOW with 'QRG-CREATE: Module size imbalance detected (ratio {ratio}). Consider redistributing functionality.' (4) INTENT COVERAGE: verify each NLU intent has at least 1 simulation scenario. If any intent uncovered: YELLOW with 'QRG-CREATE: Intent {intent} has no simulation scenario.' Record pipeline_metadata.qrg_create_results = { initial_quality, security_floor, module_balance, intent_coverage }. SKIP: for Evolve/Modify modes (use QRG1-QRG5 instead). STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=9, stage_name='QualityGate', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
QualityGate.constraints: Max 2 retries then force pass with WARNING: pipeline_metadata.quality_gate_result = 'FORCE_PASS'. EVENT EMISSION: if event_hooks configured, emit on_gate_warn event: { gate: 'QualityGate', result: 'FORCE_PASS', weighted_score, retry_count, timestamp }. test_report null -> skip to Research3 with disclaimer. AUTO-FIX DELEGATION: if quality_gate_result='FAIL' and retry_count < max_retries, before routing to ModifyStep, check if issues are auto-fixable per STANDARD.data_contracts.auto_fix_proposal categories (format/consistency/metadata/reference). If all failing issues are auto-fixable with confidence>=MEDIUM, delegate to review.AutoFixEngine instead of ModifyStep. Record auto_fix_attempted=true in pipeline_metadata.
QualityGate.Error: Unparseable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip to Research3 with WARNING, quality_gate_result='SKIP' -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

Research3.step1: [ASSERT] QualityGate executed. If false → go back to QualityGate. | CACHE CHECK (per STANDARD.PL17): compute cache_key = SHA-256(target_content_hash + 'compliance' + workspace_dir). Look up .version_history/.cache/{cache_key}.json. If found AND age < TTL (from pipeline_context.cache_policy or base TTL) AND cache_bypass=false: load cached research_context, record cache_hit in pipeline_metadata.cache_hits, skip delegation. Else: record 'Research3' in pipeline_metadata.cache_misses. Delegate to research.aisop.json — pass topic (original user topic from pipeline context), workspace_dir, research_mode='compliance'. On success: write result to cache.
Research3.step2: Collect research_context (with research_mode='compliance'). Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { research3_verified: research_context.verified_count }. MODULE_CLASSIFICATION_PERSIST: After MODULE_TYPE_DIFFERENTIATION classification is performed, persist the classification results to pipeline_metadata for cross-stage consumption. METHOD: (1) Build module_classification from Research3's MODULE_TYPE_DIFFERENTIATION analysis: for each target module file, record { type: 'primary'|'auxiliary', sub_mermaids: [string] (sub_mermaid keys if primary), called_by: [string] (calling module if auxiliary), functional_node_count: int (per FUNCTIONAL_NODE_COUNT_RULE defined in QualityGate.step2 — count of keys in functions{} WHERE key != 'endNode') }. NODE COUNT ENRICHMENT: Extend each ClassificationEntry with functional_node_count to enable SIZE-AWARE scoring in downstream consumers. METHOD: for each module file, count keys in functions{} object excluding 'endNode' entries. This count enables: (a) ThreeDimTest AUXILIARY_MODULE_TEMPLATE to apply size-proportional scoring thresholds (e.g., 3-node auxiliary modules should not be penalized equally with 30-node primary modules for missing Error fields). (b) QualityGate MODULE_LEVEL_RECHECK to cross-validate against AIAP.md declared counts without re-reading files. (c) EvolveStep structural_cap calculation to use actual node counts instead of estimating from module count. Output enrichment: pipeline_metadata.module_classification[file].functional_node_count = int. M_SCORE_INTEGRATION: Compute simplified M-score (modularity cohesion metric) per module to complement functional_node_count with a QUALITATIVE architecture signal. METHOD: (a) For each module file, scan all function step text for references to OTHER modules (patterns: 'delegate to {module}', 'run aisop.{sub}', 'pass to {module}'). Count as external_references. (b) Count total step references within the module (all 'step' mentions, function cross-references, constraint references). Count as internal_references. (c) Compute m_score = internal_references / max(1, internal_references + external_references). Higher m_score = more cohesive module (self-contained logic). Lower m_score = more coupled module (dependent on other modules). (d) INTERPRETATION: m_score >= 0.8 → HIGH cohesion (well-encapsulated). 0.5 <= m_score < 0.8 → MODERATE cohesion (normal for orchestrator modules). m_score < 0.5 → LOW cohesion (consider whether module boundaries are correct). (e) Output enrichment: pipeline_metadata.module_classification[file].m_score = float. (f) DOWNSTREAM CONSUMPTION: ThreeDimTest C2 (node load balance) MAY use m_score as weighted factor — modules with high m_score get standard C2 thresholds, modules with low m_score get relaxed C2 thresholds (orchestrator modules are expected to have more external references). (g) Log: 'M_SCORE: {module} = {m_score:.2f} ({cohesion_level})'. Log addition: include node count in summary: 'MODULE_CLASSIFICATION_PERSIST: {N} modules classified ({primary_count} primary/{p_nodes} nodes, {auxiliary_count} auxiliary/{a_nodes} nodes)'. (2) Write to pipeline_metadata.module_classification = { [module_file]: ClassificationEntry }. (3) Downstream consumers (ThreeDimTest AUXILIARY_MODULE_TEMPLATE in Generate2) SHOULD read pipeline_metadata.module_classification instead of independently classifying modules. If pipeline_metadata.module_classification is available → use it as authoritative source; if unavailable (legacy pipeline) → fall back to independent classification (backward compatible). (4) Log: 'MODULE_CLASSIFICATION_PERSIST: {N} modules classified ({primary_count} primary, {auxiliary_count} auxiliary), persisted to pipeline_metadata.' PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='Research3', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'Research3', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass research_context to ValidateStep. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=10, stage_name='Research3', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
Research3.constraints: ALWAYS delegate (unless cache hit). Focuses on regulatory compliance (GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-DSS/CCPA/EU AI Act), formal threat assessment (AT1-AT6), and standards citations (NIST/ISO). Excludes general implementation quality patterns (those belong to Research2). Output is ALWAYS named research_context per STANDARD.PL7. MODULE_TYPE_DIFFERENTIATION: When delegating to research module, pass module_classification for each target module: 'primary' (independent entry point, standalone aisop.main) or 'auxiliary' (sub_mermaid called via 'run aisop.*', no independent entry). For auxiliary modules: (a) Error field requirement downgraded from RECOMMENDED to OPTIONAL — auxiliary failure should not block pipeline. (b) Skip data compliance checks (GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-DSS) unless module explicitly handles user data (detected by file_system write + user-facing input). (c) Retain security checks (injection/permission) for all module types. Classification criteria: module is 'auxiliary' if its Mermaid graph is listed as a sub_mermaid key AND no NLU/router node routes to it directly.
Research3.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use NIST/EU AI Act general references -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ValidateStep.step1: [ASSERT] Research3 executed. If false → go back to Research3. | STAGE INPUT VALIDATION: verify draft_path from Generate2 exists and is valid JSON via file_system. If invalid → skip validation, log warning in pipeline_metadata, pass empty validation_report to SimulateStep. Read draft .aisop.json via file_system. INPUT VALIDATION: (A) Type guard — string path only. (B) Size guard — reject >500KB. (C) Injection guard — strip shell metacharacters. (D) Path guard — reject '..', validate within workspace_dir. (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize. Reject nesting >20. Execute validation checklist: STRUCTURAL (1-6) per Validate.step2, INTEGRITY (7-10) per Validate.step3, QUALITY (11-14) per Validate.step4. Additionally run COMPLIANCE (15+): if research_context.compliance_notes from Research3 provided, for each applicable requirement verify disclosure/disclaimer/safeguard language and I2/I3 guards. Score as COMPLIANT/GAP/PARTIAL. LICENSE COMPLIANCE CHECK: read target AIAP.md license field. Validate: (a) if field present, value must be valid SPDX identifier or 'proprietary' (per STANDARD license_spec). (b) if value matches protocol version pattern 'AIAP V*', flag as LICENSE_SEMANTIC_ERROR — license should be an SPDX identifier, not a protocol version. (c) if license='proprietary', verify terms_url or contact field present. (d) if target program intends aiap-store publication (check AIAP.md for store-related fields), license field is MANDATORY (PL25). Score: COMPLIANT/GAP/PARTIAL. Include in validation_result.
ValidateStep.step2: Compile validation_report per STANDARD.data_contracts.validation_report: { pass_count, total_checks, issues: [{id, severity, description}] }. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { validate_pass, validate_total }. PATTERN E IMPLICIT DEPENDENCY CHECK: if target program uses Pattern E (memory/ directory), scan all module .aisop.json files for file_system operations referencing memory/ paths. Build implicit_dependency_edges: for each file written by module A in memory/ and read by module B, add edge A→B. If any module with memory/ writes was modified in this pipeline run, flag all dependent readers as 'implicit dependency changed — verify data contract compatibility'. Report as additional MF check item. STRUCTURAL_ORPHAN_AUTO_FIX: When BOOTSTRAP_VERIFICATION_DEPTH level-2 STRUCTURAL verification identifies STRUCTURAL_ORPHAN features (output fields with no downstream consumer), generate TARGETED AUTO-FIX SUGGESTIONS instead of only reporting the orphan. PURPOSE: B6 v1.7.0 STRUCTURAL verification detects orphaned outputs but only reports them — no remediation guidance. METHOD: (1) ORPHAN DETECTION: from pipeline_metadata.bootstrap_verification_status, filter features with structural verification result containing STRUCTURAL_ORPHAN entries (output fields produced but not consumed). (2) CONSUMER SUGGESTION: for each orphaned output field, scan ALL functions across ALL modules for POTENTIAL consumers by field name matching: (a) EXACT NAME MATCH: search for the orphaned field name in other function step text. If found → high_confidence_consumer. (b) PARTIAL NAME MATCH: search for the field name's suffix or prefix (e.g., orphaned 'module_m_score' → search for 'm_score' references). If found → medium_confidence_consumer. (c) SEMANTIC MATCH: based on field semantics, identify functions that SHOULD consume this data (e.g., orphaned 'verification_depth_achieved' → ThreeDimTest quality scoring SHOULD use verification depth). If identifiable → low_confidence_consumer with rationale. (3) SUGGESTION GENERATION: for each orphan, generate targeted suggestion: 'Feature {feature} outputs {fields}. Suggested consumers: [{consumer_module}.{consumer_function}: {rationale}] (confidence: {level}).' (4) AUTO_FIX_PROPOSAL: if high_confidence_consumer found → include in pipeline_metadata.orphan_auto_fix_proposals = [{ orphaned_field, producer_feature, suggested_consumer_module, suggested_consumer_function, consumer_integration_point (which step to add consumption), confidence, auto_fixable: boolean }]. auto_fixable = true only for high_confidence matches where integration point is clear. (5) REVIEWSTEP INTEGRATION: pass orphan_auto_fix_proposals to ReviewStep for inclusion in auto_fix_proposals evaluation. High-confidence proposals → present as '[AUTO-FIX CANDIDATE]' in ReviewStep final summary. Low-confidence → present as '[MANUAL REVIEW]' advisory. (6) OUTPUT: orphan_remediation_report = { total_orphans: int, suggestions_generated: int, high_confidence: int, medium_confidence: int, low_confidence: int, auto_fix_candidates: int }. Log: 'STRUCTURAL_ORPHAN_AUTO_FIX: {total} orphans analyzed, {suggestions} suggestions generated ({high} high, {medium} medium, {low} low confidence).' SKIP: if no STRUCTURAL_ORPHAN entries in bootstrap_verification_status, or Create mode (no bootstrapped features). PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='ValidateStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'ValidateStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass validation_report to SimulateStep.
ValidateStep.step3: VERSION SYNC CHECK: read the 'version' field from all .aisop.json files in the target program AND the version field from AIAP.md. All N+1 values (N modules + 1 AIAP.md) must be identical. Any mismatch → FAIL with detailed discrepancy list: [{file, declared_version, expected_version}]. This check runs after all modifications and before governance_hash computation. EXTENDED VERSION SYNC (MF29 v3.4.0): Beyond .aisop.json version consistency, also verify: (1) AIAP.md version field matches. (2) AIAP.md module node counts match actual .aisop.json function counts. (3) AIAP.md module side_effects consistent with tool declarations. (4) quality_baseline.json version field matches (if exists) — YELLOW if mismatch. (5) agent_card.json version field matches (if exists) — YELLOW if mismatch. (6) AIAP.md quality.weighted_score matches quality_baseline.weighted_score — YELLOW if mismatch (MF30). Full metadata sync coverage: AIAP.md + all .aisop.json + agent_card.json + quality_baseline.json. MF31 MERMAID-FUNCTION CONSISTENCY (P4 v3.5.0): parse aisop.main Mermaid graph→extract all rectangle nodes (non-diamond, non-Start, non-End/endNode). For each rectangle node name, verify a matching key exists in functions{}. Missing function→YELLOW+AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: 'MF31: Mermaid node {name} has no function — remove from graph.' Reverse check: for each functions{} key, verify it appears as a node in Mermaid. Missing node→YELLOW+AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: 'MF31: function {name} not in Mermaid — add to graph.' Diamond nodes ({...?}) exempt — decisions within parent nodes. Exception: PascalCase diamonds (e.g., TypeGate, PostGate)→AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: rename to lowercase or merge into parent edges. MF31c ENDNODE NAMING (P6 v3.8.0, B5 v3.9.0 SUB-GRAPH COVERAGE): iterate over ALL aisop.* keys in each module (not just aisop.main). For each sub-graph: check terminal nodes. 'End', 'end', 'Finish', or ANY variant other than 'endNode((End))'→YELLOW+AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: 'MF31c: sub-graph {subgraph_key} terminal {name} should be endNode((End)).' This catches sub_mermaid sub-graphs (e.g., intent_dispatch using DispatchEnd((End))). MF31d FRACTAL_EXEMPT NODE COUNT (B7 v3.9.0 + B7 v1.4.0 COUNTING RULE CLARIFICATION): if module has fractal_exempt field, parse claimed node counts from text (regex: /(\d+)\s*(functional)?\s*nodes/i). Compare against actual Mermaid functional node count. FUNCTIONAL NODE DEFINITION: a Mermaid node is 'functional' if and only if a matching key exists in the functions{} object. Nodes without function definitions are excluded: (a) Start nodes with no functions{} key → excluded (e.g., pipeline sub-graph 'Start[Pipeline Entry]' with no 'Start' function). (b) Start nodes WITH a functions{} key → included (e.g., main sub-graph 'Start[User Request]' has a 'Start' function). (c) endNode((End)) → always excluded. (d) Diamond nodes without functions{} key → excluded (decisions within parent edges). COUNT METHOD: for each sub_mermaid, extract all node names from Mermaid, filter to those present in functions{}, sum across all sub_mermaid. Output count_detail: 'counted: [{node_names}], excluded: [{node_names}]' in validation_report for transparency. If claimed != actual → YELLOW: 'MF31d: fractal_exempt claims {claimed} functional nodes but actual count is {actual}. {count_detail}.' MF32 MODULE EXISTENCE VALIDATION: read agent_card.json modules[]. For each module_id, derive expected file name from last segment (e.g., 'soulbot_chat.insights' → look for file containing 'insights' in *.aisop.json listing). Scan target directory for all .aisop.json files via file_system. If any module_id has no corresponding file → RED: 'MF32: module {module_id} listed in agent_card.json but no matching .aisop.json file found on disk.' VERSION DRIFT CROSS-VALIDATION: if quality_baseline.json exists AND has evolution_history array with ≥1 entry: read evolution_history[-1].to (last evolution target version). Compare against AIAP.md version field. If mismatch → RED: 'VERSION DRIFT: quality_baseline evolution_history shows last version {history_ver} but AIAP.md declares {file_ver}. Files were not updated during prior evolutions.' This detects accumulated version drift from legacy Creator versions that didn't enforce version sync. All MF31/MF32 issues flagged as auto_fix_category='mermaid_consistency' for ReviewStep AUTO-FIX consumption.
ValidateStep.step3_5: MF33 CROSS-MODULE PARAMETER NAME DRIFT DETECTION (B1 v1.3.0 + B3 v1.4.0 CALIBRATION ENHANCEMENT — RapidFuzz normalized Levenshtein similarity; Jaro-Winkler prefix-sensitive matching; NAACL 2024 semantic drift detection): For Pattern B+ programs with 2+ modules, scan all delegation steps across all modules for parameter passing patterns. EXTRACTION: regex scan each step text for delegation patterns: 'delegate to {module} — pass {param_list}', 'pass {param} to {module}', '{module} with {param}={value}'. For each extracted delegation: (a) CALLER SIDE — extract parameter names from the delegation text. (b) CALLEE SIDE — read target module's parameters{} section, extract declared parameter names. (c) MATCH CHECK — for each caller-side param name, verify exact match exists in callee-side parameters{}. (d) ADAPTIVE FUZZY MATCH: if no exact match, apply LENGTH-ADAPTIVE THRESHOLD: (i) SHORT PARAM GUARD — if param_name length ≤ 4 characters, require EXACT MATCH ONLY (research: edit distance 1 on a 4-char string = 25% difference, unacceptable false positive rate; e.g., 'id' matching 'ip' or 'mode' matching 'node'). (ii) MEDIUM PARAMS (5-12 chars) — threshold = max(2, floor(len(param_name) * 0.25)). Use Levenshtein distance. (iii) LONG PARAMS (13+ chars) — use NORMALIZED SIMILARITY: similarity = 1 - (levenshtein_distance / (len(caller_param) + len(callee_param))). Threshold: similarity ≥ 0.75. This prevents long parameter names from having disproportionately high absolute thresholds. (iv) SYNONYM TABLE — before flagging drift, check common parameter name synonyms: { 'context'↔'ctx', 'config'↔'cfg', 'configuration'↔'config', 'params'↔'parameters', 'dir'↔'directory', 'msg'↔'message', 'info'↔'information', 'req'↔'request', 'res'↔'response', 'resp'↔'response', 'err'↔'error', 'cb'↔'callback', 'fn'↔'function', 'val'↔'value', 'idx'↔'index', 'num'↔'number', 'cnt'↔'count' }. If caller_param and callee_param are synonyms → PASS (not drift). (v) PREFIX MATCH BONUS (Jaro-Winkler inspired) — if caller_param and callee_param share a common prefix ≥ 50% of the shorter name's length (e.g., 'engagement_score' vs 'engagement_metric'), reduce severity from YELLOW to ADVISORY: 'MF33-PARAM-NEAR: {caller_param} and {callee_param} share prefix "{common_prefix}" — likely intentional variant, verify.' (vi) If fuzzy match detected (not synonym, not prefix bonus): flag as YELLOW 'MF33-PARAM-DRIFT: caller passes "{caller_param}" to {module} but callee declares "{callee_param}" (similarity: {score:.2f}) — possible rename drift'. (e) MISSING PARAM — if no exact match, no fuzzy match, and no synonym: flag as YELLOW 'MF33-PARAM-MISSING: caller passes "{caller_param}" to {module} but callee has no matching parameter declaration'. Report: { delegations_scanned: int, exact_matches: int, synonym_matches: int, prefix_near_matches: int, drift_detected: [{ caller_module, callee_module, caller_param, callee_param, similarity_score, match_method }], missing_params: [{ caller_module, callee_module, param_name }] }. FLAG ACCUMULATION LIMIT: max 50 flags per program — if exceeded, report first 50 with summary 'N additional drift/missing flags truncated'. SKIP for single-module programs or Create mode with no inter-module delegations. ValidateStep.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip validation with WARNING -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

SimulateStep.step1: [ASSERT] ValidateStep executed. If false → go back to ValidateStep. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify draft_path exists via file_system. If invalid → skip simulation, log warning in pipeline_metadata, pass empty simulation_report to ReviewStep. Delegate to simulate.aisop.json — pass target_path (draft_path), simulation_mode='full', workspace_dir, research_context (from Research3 — always named research_context per STANDARD.PL7; primarily contains compliance_notes for Category F domain scenarios), research_quality_context (from Research2 — best_practices for enriching domain-specific scenario generation; optional enhancement), evolution_context (from pipeline context for comparative scenario testing). BASELINE COMPARISON: if previous_baseline loaded by PipelineStart, pass to simulate for regression detection against stored quality scores and coverage.
SimulateStep.step2: Collect simulation_report (path coverage, dead nodes, domain scenario coverage). Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { simulate_path_coverage, simulate_scenario_coverage, simulate_dead_nodes }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='SimulateStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. SIMULATION QUALITY INDICATOR: compute from simulation_report scenario results — RED: any scenario has severity=RED → simulate_quality_indicator='RED'. YELLOW: 0 red but 3+ yellow scenarios → simulate_quality_indicator='YELLOW'. GREEN: 0 red and <=2 yellow → simulate_quality_indicator='GREEN'. Store simulate_yellow_count, simulate_red_count, simulate_quality_indicator in pipeline_metadata. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'SimulateStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain.
SimulateStep.step3: POST-SIMULATE AUTO-FIX (optional): if simulate_quality_indicator is RED and simulate_red_count <= 3, offer targeted auto-fix cycle: (a) extract RED issue descriptions from simulation_report.issues with location info, (b) delegate to modify.aisop.json with targeted fix directives (user_request = 'Fix simulation RED issues: {issue_descriptions}', research_context from Research2), (c) delegate to generate.aisop.json for rebuild + ThreeDimTest on fixed version, (d) re-run simulate.aisop.json on fixed version (max 1 auto-fix retry to prevent infinite loop). If auto-fix reduces RED to 0, update pipeline_metadata with fixed results and proceed. If RED persists after retry, proceed with original simulation_report + auto_fix_attempted=true flag. SKIP CONDITIONS: simulate_red_count > 3 (too many for targeted fix), user explicitly declines auto-fix, or Create mode with no prior quality baseline. Pass simulation_report to ObservabilityStep. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=12, stage_name='SimulateStep', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
SimulateStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. ENFORCEMENT per STANDARD.PL12: RECOMMENDED for Create, MANDATORY for Evolve (evolution introduces higher regression risk — derived reference errors and semantic inconsistencies are only detectable through simulation). Skip requires explicit pipeline_metadata.skip_reason. Auto-fix is advisory — user may decline and proceed with RED issues noted. NEW_FEATURE_FAIL_PATHS (B6 v1.5.0 + B5 v1.6.0 AUTO-EXTRACTION — Hypothesis property-based test generation from constraints; QuickCheck automatic shrinking to minimal counterexample; OWASP SPVS negative testing requirements; ICSE 2026 test adequacy for evolving systems): For Evolve mode, after main simulation completes, generate targeted FAIL and EDGE scenarios for each new feature added in evolution_plan.changes. AUTOMATIC SCENARIO EXTRACTION: Before manual scenario construction, automatically extract testable conditions from evolution_plan.changes[].description and target step text. METHOD: (1) CONDITION KEYWORD SCAN: for each LEVEL_B change description and target step constraint text, extract condition patterns using keyword matching: (a) threshold patterns: 'if {metric} >= {value}', 'when {count} > {limit}', '{field} < {threshold}' → extract { condition_type: 'threshold', metric, operator, value }. (b) existence patterns: 'if {field} exists', 'if {field} is null', 'if {file} not found' → extract { condition_type: 'existence', field, expected: true|false }. (c) state patterns: 'if {mode} = true', 'when {status} == {value}' → extract { condition_type: 'state', field, expected_value }. (2) REVERSE SCENARIO GENERATION: for each extracted condition, auto-generate the reverse scenario: threshold '>= 80%' → generate scenario with 79% (just below), existence 'if exists' → generate scenario where missing, state '== true' → generate scenario with false. (3) EDGE SCENARIO GENERATION: for each threshold condition, auto-generate boundary scenarios: exact boundary value, boundary ± 1 unit. (4) OUTPUT SEPARATION: new_feature_coverage.auto_generated_scenarios = [{ change_id, condition_extracted, reverse_scenario, edge_scenario, extraction_confidence: 'high'|'medium'|'low' }]. new_feature_coverage.manual_scenarios = [scenarios constructed by executor for conditions too complex for auto-extraction]. (5) COVERAGE COMPLETENESS: compare auto-generated count vs total LEVEL_B changes. AUTO_EXTRACTION_ADAPTIVE_THRESHOLD: Instead of fixed 50% threshold for auto-extraction coverage INFO, compute ADAPTIVE threshold from historical auto-extraction rates. METHOD: (a) Read quality_baseline.json evolution_notes for last 3 versions. Extract auto_extraction_pct from each version's simulation results (if recorded). (b) Compute adaptive_threshold = max(50, avg(last_3_versions_auto_extraction_pct) * 0.8). (c) If auto_generated < total_changes * (adaptive_threshold / 100) → INFO: 'AUTO-EXTRACT: {pct}% coverage (adaptive threshold: {adaptive_threshold}% based on {N}-version history). Manual scenario construction recommended for remaining.' (d) If history unavailable (< 3 versions or no auto_extraction_pct recorded) → fall back to fixed 50% threshold (backward compatible). (e) Record current auto_extraction_pct in pipeline_metadata for persistence to quality_baseline.json evolution_notes by ReviewStep. Log: 'AUTO_EXTRACTION_ADAPTIVE: threshold={adaptive_threshold}% (history: [{v1_pct}%, {v2_pct}%, {v3_pct}%]).' BENEFIT: as Creator matures and auto-extraction improves (v1.6.0 achieved 90%), the threshold self-calibrates upward — a drop from 90% to 60% becomes a meaningful warning instead of passing a meaningless 50% floor. If auto_generated < total_changes * 0.5 (legacy fixed threshold) → INFO (fallback) (6) Log: 'NEW_FEATURE_FAIL_PATHS: {auto} auto-extracted + {manual} manual = {total} scenarios for {changes} LEVEL_B changes.' ADAPTIVE_THRESHOLD_FEEDBACK_LOOP: After auto-extraction coverage computation, automatically persist auto_extraction_pct to pipeline_metadata for zero-maintenance quality threshold calibration. PURPOSE: AUTO_EXTRACTION_ADAPTIVE_THRESHOLD computes threshold from history but relies on auto_extraction_pct being manually recorded in evolution_notes — no automatic persistence. METHOD: (1) After computing auto_extraction_pct = (auto_generated / max(1, total_changes)) * 100 in NEW_FEATURE_FAIL_PATHS, automatically write to pipeline_metadata.auto_extraction_pct = float. (2) ReviewStep quality_baseline update (step6) SHOULD automatically include pipeline_metadata.auto_extraction_pct in evolution_notes.auto_extraction_pct — removing manual recording dependency. (3) VERIFICATION: at SimulateStep completion, check pipeline_metadata.auto_extraction_pct is set. If null → WARNING 'ADAPTIVE_FEEDBACK: auto_extraction_pct not persisted — adaptive threshold will use fallback in next evolution.' (4) Set pipeline_metadata.auto_extraction_pct_persisted = true. Log: 'ADAPTIVE_FEEDBACK: auto_extraction_pct={pct}% auto-persisted to pipeline_metadata for ReviewStep quality_baseline inclusion.' MANUAL FALLBACK METHOD: (1) For each LEVEL_B change not covered by auto-extraction, extract the NEW logic it introduces (new constraints, new checks, new thresholds).
