(a) For each non-orchestrator module, scan function text (all steps, constraints) for references to fields or function names belonging to other modules. Detection patterns: module_name references (e.g., 'SimulateStep output', 'ReviewStep result'), shared field names that originate from another module's output. (b) Build discovered_dependency_graph: { module_a -> module_b: [shared_field_1, shared_field_2] }. (c) Compare with declared dependencies from module_classification in system content. (d) If discovered dependencies not in declared -> classify as 'undeclared': ADVISORY: 'Discovered {N} undeclared dependencies: [{module_a->module_b via field_x}, ...]. Consider updating module_classification for accurate impact prediction.' (e) If declared dependencies not found in actual text -> classify as 'stale': ADVISORY: 'Declared dependency {module_a->module_b} not found in code — may be outdated.' Persist dependency_divergence_report: { version, undeclared: N, stale: M, graph_accuracy_pct }. ORPHAN_AUTO_FIX_APPLICATION: After m_score persistence, evaluate and optionally APPLY orphan auto-fix proposals from ValidateStep. PURPOSE: STRUCTURAL_ORPHAN_AUTO_FIX generates consumer suggestions for orphaned bootstrap features but only creates proposals — no mechanism actually APPLIES the wiring. The detection→remediation loop is open. METHOD: (1) PROPOSAL LOAD: read pipeline_metadata.orphan_auto_fix_proposals (populated by ValidateStep B10 v1.8.0). Filter entries with auto_fixable=true AND confidence='high'. (2) DIFF PREVIEW: for each eligible proposal: (a) read the suggested consumer module file, (b) locate the integration point (step identified in proposal.suggested_consumer_step), (c) generate a diff preview showing the proposed addition of a reference to the orphaned field in that step's text. Format: '+ {orphaned_field} from {producer_module} consumed at {consumer_step}'. (3) USER APPROVAL: present diff previews to user: 'ORPHAN AUTO-FIX: {N} high-confidence proposals ready for application. | # | Orphaned Field | Producer | Suggested Consumer | Diff Preview |'. User actions per proposal: [Apply] → execute fix, [Skip] → defer to next evolution, [Reject] → mark as rejected (won't be proposed again). (4) APPLICATION: for approved proposals, apply the wiring change to the consumer module's step text. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename), backup existing file as .bak. (5) VERIFICATION: after applying each fix, perform quick structural verification — check that the modified consumer step's references are syntactically valid and the orphaned field name matches the producer's output field exactly. If verification fails → ROLLBACK the change and report failure. (6) OUTPUT: orphan_fix_application = { proposals_evaluated: int, eligible_high_confidence: int, user_approved: int, applied: int, verified_no_regression: int, rolled_back: int, deferred: int, rejected: int }. Log: 'ORPHAN_AUTO_FIX_APPLICATION: {evaluated} proposals evaluated, {eligible} eligible, {approved} approved, {applied} applied ({verified} verified, {rolled_back} rolled back).' SKIP: if no orphan_auto_fix_proposals in pipeline_metadata (no orphans detected or ValidateStep didn't run), or Create mode (no prior features to be orphaned), or all proposals have confidence < 'high'. DIRECTION_DECOMPOSITION_HISTORY: In DIRECTION_CONTINUITY_SCORE evaluation, when a direction was DECOMPOSED by B7 v1.9.0, evaluate EACH SUB-DIRECTION independently. PURPOSE: compound directions are split into sub-directions but effectiveness is only tracked at the compound level — this masks sub-component failures. METHOD: (1) DECOMPOSITION CHECK: for each direction being evaluated in continuity scoring, check pipeline_context.decomposed_directions (populated by EvolveStep B7 v1.9.0). If direction was decomposed → has sub_directions array. (2) SUB-DIRECTION EVALUATION: for each sub-direction, independently compute effectiveness against its mapped dimension (same method as standard DIRECTION_CONTINUITY_SCORE). (3) MIXED CLASSIFICATION: if sub-directions have inconsistent results (e.g., 2 EFFECTIVE + 1 COUNTERPRODUCTIVE), classify compound direction as 'MIXED' instead of averaging. (4) GRANULAR STRATEGY MEMORY: store sub-direction effectiveness in evolution_strategy_memory alongside compound direction. Format: { compound_direction, classification: 'MIXED', sub_directions: [{ name, effectiveness, classification }] }. (5) DOWNSTREAM: EvolveStep STRATEGY_ANALYTICS_ACTIONABLE can selectively retain EFFECTIVE sub-directions and exclude COUNTERPRODUCTIVE ones from compound directions. (6) OUTPUT: decomposed_effectiveness = [{ compound_direction: string, was_decomposed: boolean, sub_directions: [{ name: string, category: string, effectiveness: float, classification: string }], compound_classification: 'EFFECTIVE'|'NEUTRAL'|'COUNTERPRODUCTIVE'|'MIXED' }]. Log: 'DECOMPOSITION_HISTORY: {evaluated} directions checked, {decomposed} were decomposed, {mixed} classified as MIXED.' SKIP: if no decomposed_directions in pipeline_context (no compound directions in this evolution), or Create mode. STRATEGY_MEMORY_PRUNING: After DIRECTION_DECOMPOSITION_HISTORY, apply evolution_strategy_memory growth management. (a) In direction_effectiveness_history, keep the most recent 5 versions in full detail. For entries older than 5 versions → compact to summary: { version, effective_count, neutral_count, counterproductive_count, top_effective_categories: [top 3 by effectiveness] }. (b) In proven_approaches, merge entries referencing the same category into a single consolidated entry: { category, consolidated_evidence: [all evidence strings], first_proven_version, approach_count }. (c) Target: total strategy_memory entries (direction_history + proven + anti_patterns) ≤ 50. If over limit → prune oldest compacted summaries first. Output: pruning_summary { pruned_versions: N, merged_approaches: M, current_total_entries: K, within_limit: bool }. PRUNING_EFFECTIVENESS_AUDIT: After STRATEGY_MEMORY_PRUNING, verify pruning did not remove data needed by current-version analyses. (a) Record all pruned detail keys: { pruned_versions: [version_ids], pruned_fields: [field_names] }. (b) After all ReviewStep analyses complete, check if any analysis (ObservabilityStep trend tracking, EvolveStep strategy consumption, ReviewStep direction effectiveness) attempted to access a pruned version's detailed data. Detection method: if an analysis references a version_id that was pruned AND the analysis result contains 'insufficient_data' or 'skipped' for that version -> classify as access_miss. (c) If access_miss > 0 -> ADVISORY: 'Pruning removed {N} detail(s) accessed by {analysis_names}. Consider extending retention window from 5 to {5+miss_count} versions.' (d) If access_miss = 0 for 3+ consecutive versions -> 'Pruning policy validated: no analysis disrupted in {N} versions.' Persist pruning_audit_history: { version, access_misses, advisory_issued }. DIRECTION_CONTINUITY_SCORE: After direction archival, compute EFFECTIVENESS SCORE for archived directions from version N-1 to assess whether past evolution directions ACTUALLY IMPROVED the targeted dimensions. PURPOSE: B4 v1.6.0 archives directions to evolution_history but provides no feedback on whether those directions were effective — without outcome verification, the evolution strategy operates open-loop. METHOD: (1) DIRECTION HISTORY LOAD: read evolution_history[version_N-1].directions (archived by previous evolution). If unavailable → SKIP with INFO 'No prior version directions to evaluate.' (2) DIMENSION MAPPING: map each direction's category to ThreeDimTest dimensions: 'architecture'/'structure'/'complexity' → Cognitive (C), 'quality'/'testing'/'verification'/'scoring' → Detail (D), 'security'/'safety'/'integrity'/'governance' → Intrinsic (I), 'efficiency'/'performance'/'optimization' → Cognitive (C), 'data_flow'/'contracts'/'observability' → Detail (D). (3) EFFECTIVENESS COMPUTATION: for each direction from version N-1, compare the mapped dimension's score BEFORE (version N-1 quality_baseline) and AFTER (current version score). effectiveness = dimension_score_after - dimension_score_before. Normalize by number of changes invested: effectiveness_per_change = effectiveness / max(1, changes_targeting_this_direction). (4) CLASSIFICATION: effectiveness > 0.05 → 'EFFECTIVE' (measurable improvement). -0.05 <= effectiveness <= 0.05 → 'NEUTRAL' (no measurable impact). effectiveness < -0.05 → 'COUNTERPRODUCTIVE' (dimension regressed). (5) ADVISORY: COUNTERPRODUCTIVE directions → ADVISORY: 'DIRECTION_CONTINUITY: direction "{direction}" from v{N-1} was COUNTERPRODUCTIVE — {dimension} dropped by {delta}. Consider different approach in next evolution.' NEUTRAL directions across 2+ consecutive versions → ADVISORY: 'DIRECTION_CONTINUITY: direction "{direction}" has been NEUTRAL for {N} versions — consider deprioritizing or reformulating.' (6) Output: direction_continuity = { evaluated_directions: [{ direction, category, mapped_dimension, score_before, score_after, effectiveness, effectiveness_per_change, classification }], effective_count: int, neutral_count: int, counterproductive_count: int, overall_strategy_health: 'healthy'|'mixed'|'ineffective' }. Include in pipeline_metadata and display in ReviewStep final summary.
ReviewStep.step4: POST-PIPELINE MANDATORY OUTPUT AUDIT (per MF17): Before computing governance_hash (PL14): (1) Enumerate mandatory output list per STANDARD MF17: AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, agent_card.json (UNCONDITIONAL for all patterns), .version_history/{version}/meta.json. For Evolve mode additionally: .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json. (2) For each mandatory output: verify file exists in target program directory via file_system. (3) Missing agent_card.json → attempt auto-generation from AIAP.md metadata: extract name, version, description, capabilities.offered, tools → generate minimal agent_card.json with safety_card stub (risk_level derived from trust_level). (3_5) QUALITY BASELINE AUTO-SCAFFOLDING: Missing quality_baseline.json → attempt auto-generation from pipeline data. PURPOSE: instead of blocking the pipeline when quality_baseline.json is absent (common for v1.0.0 programs or programs created before MF17 enforcement), auto-generate a valid baseline from the current pipeline's ThreeDimTest and SimulateStep results. METHOD: (a) EXTRACT SCORES: from test_report (produced by Generate2 ThreeDimTest): weighted_score, grade, C/I/D scores, MF check results, PL check results. From simulation_report (produced by SimulateStep): total_scenarios, passed, failed, pass_rate, category breakdown. (b) BUILD SCAFFOLD: quality_baseline = { scoring_scale: '1-5', version: current pipeline version, snapshot_date: current ISO date, three_dim_test: { weighted_score, grade, cognitive: { weight, score, notes }, intrinsic: { weight, score, na_items, notes }, detail: { weight, score, notes }, multi_file: MF summary string, pipeline: PL summary string }, simulation: { total_scenarios, passed, failed, pass_rate, categories: category_breakdown, result_summary: { GREEN, YELLOW, RED counts } }, regression_check: { QRG1: 'N/A — first baseline', QRG3: { score, floor: 3.0, result } }, evolution_notes: { ['v' + version + '_changes']: ['Initial quality baseline auto-generated by Creator pipeline'] } }. (c) CREATE MODE HANDLING: for Create mode (no prior version), all fields populated from current pipeline results. No regression_check needed (first version). (d) EVOLVE MODE HANDLING: for Evolve mode, quality_baseline.json SHOULD exist (from PL26 snapshot or prior pipeline). If missing → WARNING 'quality_baseline.json missing in Evolve mode — auto-generating from current results. Prior version baselines unavailable for QRG regression check.' Generate scaffold but mark QRG1 as 'DEGRADED — no prior baseline available.' (e) WRITE: atomic write to target_program_path/quality_baseline.json. Log: 'MF17 AUTO-SCAFFOLD: quality_baseline.json generated from pipeline results (score: {score}/{grade}, {scenarios} scenarios).' (f) VERIFICATION: re-read generated file, parse JSON, verify version matches current. (4) Re-check after auto-generation. If ANY mandatory output still missing → BLOCK finalization: 'Missing mandatory output: {file_name}. Pipeline cannot compute governance_hash with incomplete outputs per MF17. Run pipeline stage {stage_name} to generate missing output.' (5) Record pipeline_metadata.mandatory_output_audit = { checked: [...], present: [...], auto_generated: [...], missing: [...], result: 'PASS' | 'BLOCK' }.
ReviewStep.step5: GOVERNANCE HASH (per STANDARD.PL14): compute SHA-256 of all .aisop.json AND .aisip.json file contents in target directory, concatenated in alphabetical filename order. DUAL MODE: when target_format='DUAL' or .aisip.json files exist in target directory, include both format files in hash computation: sorted(*.aisop.json + *.aisip.json). This ensures governance integrity covers all program representations. Write computed hash to AIAP.md governance_hash field. GOVERNANCE HASH VERIFICATION: after computing SHA-256 governance_hash and writing it to AIAP.md, perform verification: re-read all .aisop.json files, recompute SHA-256, compare with written value. If mismatch → WARN 'governance_hash verification failed — possible concurrent file modification' and retry hash computation once. If retry also mismatches → flag in pipeline_metadata.hash_verification_failed=true. STEPS SUMMARY: Run aisop.steps_summary — pass stage_number=15, stage_name='ReviewStep', stage_status, key_outputs, issues_found, next_stage.
ReviewStep.step6: ROOT QUALITY_BASELINE UPDATE: in addition to writing .version_history/{version}/quality_baseline.json, also write/overwrite the root quality_baseline.json with the latest version's scores. This root file is the active baseline used by future pipeline runs for regression detection (QRG1). Content: same as version snapshot but with version field updated to current. PIPELINE_METADATA_COMPRESSION: Before persisting pipeline_metadata to quality_baseline, separate RUNTIME-ONLY fields from ARCHIVAL fields to reduce persistent metadata size. PURPOSE: pipeline_metadata accumulates data from all 16 stages — with SCHEMA_MIGRATION_VERSIONING, CROSS_EVOLUTION_LEARNING_PERSISTENCE, and growing bootstrap_advisory, the metadata object grows unboundedly across evolutions. No compression or selective persistence means quality_baseline.json may become unwieldy. METHOD: (1) FIELD CLASSIFICATION: classify each pipeline_metadata field as RUNTIME or ARCHIVAL: RUNTIME_ONLY (not persisted to quality_baseline, only used during pipeline execution): [attestation_chain, cache_hits, cache_misses, module_hashes, stage_timings_raw, contract_violations, schema_migration_applied, token_tracking_raw, gate_result_details, auto_fix_proposals_raw, sampling_budget, incremental_decisions]. ARCHIVAL (persisted to quality_baseline for cross-version analysis): [weighted_score, grade, dimension_scores, simulation_coverage, scenario_counts, evolution_notes, bootstrap_advisory, cap_utilization, evolution_strategy_memory, module_metrics, reconciliation_history, direction_continuity, auto_extraction_pct, version_sync]. (2) NESTED COMPRESSION: for archival fields that contain verbose nested data, compress to summary form: (a) full impact_chains → summary: { total_chains: int, max_depth: int, high_risk_count: int } (discard individual chain details), (b) full change_registry → summary: { total_changes: int, structural: int, internal: int, affected_modules: [string] } (discard per-change descriptions), (c) full pre_flight_report → summary: { pass: boolean, collisions: int, conflicts: int } (discard detailed entries). (3) SIZE ESTIMATION: estimate pre-compression and post-compression sizes: pre_size = JSON.stringify(full_metadata).length, post_size = JSON.stringify(archival_only).length, compression_ratio = post_size / pre_size. (4) OUTPUT: metadata_compression = { original_fields: int, runtime_excluded: int, archival_preserved: int, nested_compressed: int, original_size_estimate: int, compressed_size_estimate: int, compression_ratio: float, runtime_fields_excluded: [string], archival_fields_preserved: [string] }. Log: 'METADATA_COMPRESSION: {original} fields → {archival} archival ({runtime} runtime excluded, {nested} nested compressed). Size: {original_size}→{compressed_size} chars ({ratio:.1%} reduction).' (5) WRITE: persist only archival data to quality_baseline.json. Runtime data remains in pipeline_metadata for the current pipeline's ObservabilityStep and ReviewStep consumption but is NOT written to persistent storage. TARGET: reduce persisted metadata size by 40-60% while preserving all cross-version analysis capability. SKIP: Create mode (first version, no compression benefit), or pipeline_metadata has < 10 fields (too small for meaningful compression). COMPRESSION_ADAPTIVE_POLICY: After PIPELINE_METADATA_COMPRESSION, apply adaptive field reclassification based on cross-version usage patterns. (a) For each ARCHIVAL field, check access_history: if field was not read by any cross-version analysis (ObservabilityStep, ReviewStep trend tracking, EvolveStep strategy consumption) in the last 3 versions → reclassify as RUNTIME_ONLY. (b) For each RUNTIME_ONLY field, check recovery_history: if field was manually recovered or re-requested by a user/pipeline step in 2+ of the last 3 versions → reclassify as ARCHIVAL. (c) Persist adaptive_field_classification to quality_baseline: { version, downgraded: [{ field, reason, last_accessed_version }], upgraded: [{ field, reason, recovery_count }], unchanged: N }. (d) Report reclassification_summary. If no fields meet reclassification criteria → report: 'Compression policy stable — no reclassification needed.' TOKEN_COMPRESSION_EXECUTION: After COMPRESSION_ADAPTIVE_POLICY, when Y2 compression_candidates are generated by AUTONOMOUS_YELLOW_RESOLUTION, enhance with quantified analysis. (a) Scan all module constraint text for repeated_patterns: identify text fragments >50 characters that appear in 3+ locations across modules. (b) For each pattern: compute occurrences, affected_modules, char_count_per_occurrence, total_chars = occurrences × char_count. (c) Generate compression_plan: { patterns: [{ text_preview: first 80 chars, occurrences, modules_affected, suggested_template_name, estimated_token_savings: total_chars / 4 }], total_estimated_savings, savings_pct: total_estimated_savings / total_module_tokens × 100 }. (d) If savings_pct > 10% → RECOMMENDATION: 'Token compression can save ~{savings_pct}% tokens ({total_estimated_savings} tokens). Top 3 patterns: [{pattern_list}].' If savings_pct <= 10% → 'Token compression potential minimal ({savings_pct}%) — structural changes may be more effective for Y2 resolution.' Advisory only — do not auto-apply. CONSTRAINT_TEMPLATE_SPECIFICATION: After TOKEN_COMPRESSION_EXECUTION evaluation, generate constraint_template_catalog for systematic token reduction. (a) Scan all constraint text in main.aisop.json — identify repeated patterns: 'After [FEATURE_NAME],' prefix (count occurrences), 'If [condition] → [action]' conditional (count occurrences), version check patterns 'if cap_utilization_history contains N+ versions' (count occurrences), classification patterns 'classify as X/Y/Z' (count occurrences). (b) For each identified pattern with 3+ occurrences: define template_spec: { template_id, pattern_regex, parameterized_form: '$ref:template_id(PARAM1, PARAM2)', occurrences_count, estimated_token_savings }. (c) Compute total_compression_ratio = sum(token_savings) / total_constraint_tokens. (d) Define template_format_spec: { syntax: '$ref:template_id(params)', expansion_rule: 'replace $ref with full text substituting params', validation: 'expanded text must match original semantics' }. (e) Persist constraint_template_catalog to quality_baseline with: { templates_identified, total_occurrences, compression_ratio_estimate, format_spec, implementation_readiness }. If compression_ratio > 0.15 → ADVISORY: 'Template system can reduce tokens by {pct}%. Ready for implementation in v1.14.0.' SEMANTIC_COMPRESSION_ANALYSIS: After CONSTRAINT_TEMPLATE_SPECIFICATION catalog generation, perform deeper semantic-level compression analysis beyond simple pattern matching. (a) FUNCTIONAL_EQUIVALENCE_SCAN: identify constraint text segments across different steps that express the same logical operation with different wording (e.g., 'check if X exists and is valid' vs 'verify X presence and validity'). Count functionally_equivalent_pairs. (b) VERSION_BLOAT_ANALYSIS: compute constraint text growth per version from v1.6.0 to current. For each B-change added since v1.6.0, measure chars_added. Compute bloat_rate = total_chars_added / number_of_b_changes. If bloat_rate > avg_constraint_length × 1.5 → newer constraints are verbose relative to original style. (c) COMPILATION_FEASIBILITY: evaluate whether older version constraints (v1.6.0-v1.9.0 era) can be 'compiled' into more compact imperative form without semantic loss. Example: a 500-char constraint describing a 3-step process could be expressed as '(1) check X (2) compute Y (3) persist Z' in ~100 chars. Estimate compilation_ratio = compiled_length / original_length for a sample of 10 oldest constraints. (d) Compute deep_compression_potential = (functionally_equivalent_savings + compilation_savings) / total_constraint_tokens. (e) If deep_compression_potential > 0.15 → ADVISORY: 'Deep semantic compression can reduce tokens by {pct}%. Recommend executing in v1.15.0.' Persist semantic_compression_analysis to quality_baseline. CREATION EXPERIENCE (per STANDARD.data_contracts.creation_experience): compile pipeline learning record from accumulated pipeline_metadata: (a) challenges — extract stages with retries, fallbacks, or QualityGate failures from pipeline_metadata, (b) quality_outcome — weighted_score + grade + dimension_scores from test_report, (c) simulation_highlights — coverage + dead_nodes + red_issues from simulation_report, (d) key_decisions — LEVEL_A accept/reject decisions from EvolveStep, (e) research_insights — top 3 verified_findings by relevance from research_context. Persist to .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Record pipeline_metadata.creation_experience_written = true. This is the final step before pipeline completion. DIRECTION_SLIM: Generate only HIGH and MEDIUM priority directions. Omit LOW priority items that report 'no impact' or 'continued operation' — these are purely mechanical and provide no actionable information.
ReviewStep.step7: DESCRIPTION REFRESH(C3): LEVEL_B changes→verify (1) AIAP.md summary mentions new capabilities(missing→YELLOW), (2) modified modules' summary/description reflect changes(unchanged→YELLOW), (3) AIAP_cn.md exists→advisory for localization update. Advisory-only. MODULE METADATA SYNC: per module re-read .aisop.json→verify nodes count, side_effects vs tools, idempotent vs behavior(file_system write→false unless justified), critical flag. Auto-correct mismatches+log corrections. MF29 VERSION SYNC (P1 v3.4.0): verify version equality across all metadata files: AIAP.md.version == main.aisop.json.version == agent_card.json.version == quality_baseline.json.version. Any mismatch→RED: 'MF29 FAIL: version mismatch {file}={ver}, expected {expected}'. Auto-correct all to AIAP.md version. MF30 SCORE CONSISTENCY (P2 v3.4.0): verify AIAP.md.quality.weighted_score == quality_baseline.three_dim_test.weighted_score. Mismatch→YELLOW: 'MF30: score inconsistency AIAP.md={x} vs baseline={y}'. Auto-correct AIAP.md to baseline value (baseline is authoritative). TAGS LIMIT AUDIT (P3 v3.4.0): if AIAP.md tags count>30→AUTO-FIX: remove internal implementation tags (step-compression,dry-constraints,inline-trace,semantic-compression,node-merge,shared-constraints), merge synonyms, keep user-facing terms, cap at 30. Auto-sync agent_card.json tags to match. Log: 'TAGS AUTO-FIX: {old}→{new} ({removed} compressed)'. KEYWORDS LIMIT AUDIT (P7 v3.5.0): if AIAP.md discovery_keywords count>20→AUTO-FIX: remove entries duplicated in tags[], remove internal terms, cap at 20. Log: 'KEYWORDS AUTO-FIX: {old}→{new} ({deduped} removed)'. MF31 MERMAID AUDIT (P4 v3.5.0): per ValidateStep MF31. AUTO-FIX: (a) function exists but not in Mermaid→add node to Mermaid graph at correct topological position with edges to/from adjacent nodes. (b) Mermaid rectangle node has no function→remove from Mermaid graph, reconnect edges. (c) PascalCase diamond suggesting independent node→rename to lowercase decision label (e.g., TypeGate{leaf/branch?}→type?{leaf/branch?} or merge into parent output edges). MF31c ENDNODE NAMING (P6 v3.8.0, B5 v3.9.0): iterate over ALL aisop.* keys in each module (not just aisop.main — includes aisop.intent_dispatch, aisop.sub_*, etc.). For each sub-graph: scan Mermaid for terminal nodes. If terminal node uses 'End', 'end', 'Finish', or any variant other than 'endNode((End))'→AUTO-FIX: replace with 'endNode((End))' in Mermaid graph. Log: 'MF31c: {sub_graph}.terminal {old}→endNode((End)) normalized.' Log: 'MF31 AUTO-FIX: {added} nodes added, {removed} stale nodes removed, {renamed} diamonds renamed, {endnode_fixed} endNode normalized.'
ReviewStep.step8: FINAL ADJUSTED SCORE (INS-005): After ReviewStep.Finalize updates AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, and agent_card.json to v{new_version}, re-evaluate D6 (version sync), D8 (agent card completeness), and D10 (quality baseline sync) using the NOW-CURRENT files. Compute final_adjusted_score = C_avg × 0.25 + I_avg × 0.45 + D_adjusted_avg × 0.30 where D_adjusted_avg replaces the Stage 8 D6/D8/D10 scores with the post-Finalize re-scored values. Record in pipeline_metadata: { stage8_score: {original}, final_adjusted_score: {adjusted}, adjustment_delta: {delta} }. Report both scores to user: 'Stage 8 score: {X}, Final adjusted score: {Y} (D6/D8/D10 re-scored after ReviewStep file updates)'. If final_adjusted_score < stage8_score, emit WARNING — this should never happen (ReviewStep only adds correct data). DEFERRED_SCORE_FINAL_RECONCILIATION: After computing final_adjusted_score, VERIFY that each deferred item's projected_score (from DEFERRED_SCORE_MARKING B2 v1.7.0) matches the actual post-ReviewStep score. PURPOSE: DEFERRED_SCORE_MARKING projects D6/D9 scores but never verifies the projection was correct — if ReviewStep fails to sync versions or compute hash, projected_score ≠ actual_score, and the mid-pipeline projected score becomes a lie. METHOD: (1) LOAD DEFERRED ITEMS: read test_report.deferred_items (populated by ThreeDimTest step5 DEFERRED_SCORE_MARKING). Each entry contains: { item_id (e.g., 'D6', 'D9'), raw_score, projected_score, resolved_by }. (2) ACTUAL SCORE EXTRACTION: for each deferred item, re-score the item using post-ReviewStep files: D6 (version sync) → check all files now have matching versions after VERSION_SYNC_MANIFEST. D9 (governance hash) → check governance_hash is now computed and embedded in AIAP.md. Score each using same ThreeDimTest criteria as original scoring. (3) RECONCILIATION: for each deferred item, compare projected_score vs actual_post_review_score: if match (projected == actual) → RECONCILED: 'DEFERRED_RECONCILIATION: {item_id} projected {projected} → actual {actual}. Projection was correct.' if mismatch (projected != actual) → WARNING: 'DEFERRED_RECONCILIATION: {item_id} projected {projected} but actual is {actual}. Deferred scoring assumption was incorrect — ReviewStep did not fully resolve this item. Delta: {delta}.' (4) SCORE IMPACT: if any deferred item has actual < projected → recompute final_adjusted_score using actual scores instead of projected. This may lower the final score. Emit: 'DEFERRED_RECONCILIATION_IMPACT: final_adjusted_score corrected from {projected_final} to {reconciled_final} based on actual deferred item scores.' (5) OUTPUT: deferred_reconciliation = { items_checked: int, reconciled: int, mismatched: int, score_impact: float (delta between projected and reconciled final scores), entries: [{ item_id, projected_score, actual_score, status: 'RECONCILED'|'MISMATCHED', delta }] }. Record in pipeline_metadata. (6) Log: 'DEFERRED_RECONCILIATION: {checked} items checked, {reconciled} reconciled, {mismatched} mismatched. Score impact: {impact}.' SKIP: if no deferred_items in test_report (no DEFERRED_SCORE_MARKING applied, e.g., Create mode or mid-pipeline ThreeDimTest not yet executed). RECONCILIATION_TREND_TRACKING: After reconciliation, PERSIST deferred_reconciliation results to quality_baseline.json for cross-version accuracy tracking and calibration. PURPOSE: DEFERRED_SCORE_FINAL_RECONCILIATION verifies projected vs actual but doesn't track accuracy ACROSS VERSIONS — if projections are consistently wrong, the deferred scoring mechanism itself needs recalibration, but there's no historical data to detect this. METHOD: (1) PERSIST CURRENT RESULTS: write deferred_reconciliation to quality_baseline.json under reconciliation_history: append { version: current_version, items_checked: int, reconciled: int, mismatched: int, score_impact: float, projection_accuracy_pct: (reconciled / max(1, items_checked)) * 100 }. (2) TREND ANALYSIS: if reconciliation_history has 3+ entries, compute rolling projection_accuracy_pct across versions. If accuracy drops below 80% for 2+ consecutive versions → ADVISORY: 'RECONCILIATION_TREND: deferred score projections have been inaccurate in {N} of last {M} versions (accuracy: {pct}%). Projection assumptions may need revision — consider: (a) adding more deferred items to increase sample size, (b) revising which items qualify for deferral, (c) checking if ReviewStep is consistently failing to resolve deferred conditions.' (3) CALIBRATION HEALTH: classify reconciliation health: accuracy >= 90% for 3+ versions → 'well_calibrated', accuracy 80-89% → 'adequate', accuracy < 80% → 'needs_recalibration'. (4) OUTPUT: reconciliation_trend = { history_entries: int, current_accuracy_pct: float, rolling_accuracy_pct: float, calibration_health: 'well_calibrated'|'adequate'|'needs_recalibration', trend: 'improving'|'stable'|'degrading', advisory_emitted: boolean }. Log: 'RECONCILIATION_TREND: accuracy={current_accuracy}% (rolling={rolling}%), health={calibration_health}, trend={trend}.' SKIP: if no deferred_reconciliation data (Create mode, or first evolution with deferred scoring). RECONCILIATION_AUTO_RECALIBRATION: When RECONCILIATION_TREND_TRACKING detects calibration_health = 'needs_recalibration', AUTOMATICALLY ADJUST deferred score projection assumptions. PURPOSE: B3 v1.9.0 detects when projections are inaccurate but only emits advisory — no mechanism adjusts projection assumptions, so the same inaccurate projections repeat. METHOD: (1) TRIGGER: read reconciliation_trend from current pipeline execution. If calibration_health = 'needs_recalibration' (accuracy < 80% for 2+ versions): (2) HISTORICAL MISMATCH ANALYSIS: compute historical_mismatch_delta = mean(projected_score - actual_score) across all mismatched items in reconciliation_history. Compute mismatch_stddev = stddev of deltas. (3) PROJECTION ADJUSTMENT: for future deferred items, adjust: adjusted_projected_score = raw_projected_score - abs(historical_mismatch_delta). Add uncertainty_margin = max(mismatch_stddev, 0.2). Report as RANGE instead of point estimate: '{item_id} projected {low:.1f}-{high:.1f} (calibration uncertainty ±{margin:.2f})' where low = adjusted - margin, high = adjusted + margin. (4) PERSISTENCE: write calibration_adjustment to quality_baseline.json: { applied_version: current_version, adjustment_delta: float, uncertainty_margin: float, trigger_accuracy: float, method: 'historical_mismatch_correction' }. (5) DOWNSTREAM: next evolution's DEFERRED_SCORE_MARKING reads calibration_adjustment and applies pre-adjusted projections. (6) RECOVERY: if reconciliation accuracy improves to >= 90% in subsequent version, reset calibration_adjustment (remove from quality_baseline) and log: 'RECALIBRATION_RECOVERED: accuracy restored to {pct}%, reverting to standard projections.' (7) OUTPUT: recalibration_applied = { triggered: boolean, previous_accuracy: float, adjustment_delta: float, uncertainty_margin: float, items_adjusted: int, recovery_eligible: boolean }. Log: 'RECONCILIATION_RECALIBRATION: accuracy={accuracy}%, delta={delta}, margin={margin}, {items} items adjusted.' SKIP: if calibration_health != 'needs_recalibration', or reconciliation_history has < 3 entries (insufficient data for calibration).
ReviewStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Review has full context from all pipeline stages.
ReviewStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present draft directly -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

ModifyPipeline.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION + INIT: resolve target AISOP file (same logic as PipelineStart Evolve mode). Initialize pipeline_metadata = { pipeline_run_id: UUID_v4, pipeline_type: 'modify' }.
ModifyPipeline.step2: EXECUTE MODIFY PIPELINE SEQUENCE: delegate each stage sequentially, accumulating pipeline_metadata throughout: (a) Research(quality) — research.aisop.json with research_mode='quality', (b) Modify — modify.aisop.json with target_file + research_context, (c) Generate — generate.aisop.json for rebuild + ThreeDimTest, (d) Validate — inline structural + integrity + quality checks per STANDARD validation checklist, (e) Simulate (RECOMMENDED) — simulate.aisop.json if not skipped (record skip_reason if skipped), (f) PostSimulateGate (RECOMMENDED, after Simulate) — evaluate simulate_quality_indicator per STANDARD.PL13. GREEN: proceed. YELLOW: warn + proceed. RED: offer auto-fix or user override per PostSimulateGate logic. Skip if Simulate was skipped. (g) Review — review.aisop.json with all accumulated reports.
ModifyPipeline.step3: FINALIZE: on ReviewStep completion, pipeline complete. Apply same AIAP.md update and governance_hash logic as full pipeline ReviewStep.step3.
ModifyPipeline.constraints: Shorter pipeline: Research(quality)→Modify→Generate→Validate→[Simulate]→[PostSimulateGate]→Review. No Evolve step. No Research(structure/compliance). Version bump follows STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management: modify_only→patch+1.
ModifyPipeline.Error: Any step fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present partial results -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

Validate.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | STANDALONE MODE: Read .aisop.json via file_system. Reject >500KB or nesting >20.
Validate.step2: STRUCTURAL (1-6): protocol/id/name/version/summary/description, tools array, parameters, system_prompt, instruction, aisop.main Mermaid, functions.
Validate.step3: INTEGRITY (7-10): matching Mermaid<->functions, Error fields present, endNode reachable, no orphan edges.
Validate.step4: QUALITY (11-18): C2 node load, C4 data flow, I1 tool honesty, I2 security guards (with zero-tool adaptation per STANDARD I2), I4 circuit-breaker pattern, I6 resilience + semantic accuracy, D4 consistency, D5 boundary rules. NOTE: for comprehensive quality testing including all C/I/D dimensions, use full pipeline ThreeDimTest.
Validate.step5: Suggest: (1) run full pipeline (Create/Evolve) for comprehensive improvement, (2) run Modify pipeline for targeted fixes.
Validate.constraints: Read-only. No domain research in standalone mode. Show fixes, not just failures.
Validate.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: specific error + minimal fix suggestion -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

SimulateFlow.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | STANDALONE MODE: Delegate to simulate.aisop.json — pass target, mode, workspace_dir.
SimulateFlow.step2: Collect simulation_report. Suggest: (1) run full pipeline for improvement, (2) run Modify pipeline for fixes.
SimulateFlow.constraints: Read-only. ALWAYS delegate. No domain research in standalone mode.
SimulateFlow.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: static Mermaid path trace -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

Compare.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: accept two target paths (AISOP files or directories). If only one provided, prompt for the second. Resolve each path using PipelineStart logic (exact path, name scan, numbered list). Support: (a) two versions of same AISOP (e.g., v1.0.0.bak vs current), (b) two different AISOPs.
Compare.step2: Read both AISOP files via file_system. For multi-file (Pattern B+), read all sub-modules. Extract: Pattern, version, node count, tools[], parameters, NLU intents (if any), function names, constraint patterns. MULTI-FILE DIFF: for Pattern B+ programs (multiple .aisop.json files), generate per-module diff matrix: | Module | AISOP A Nodes | AISOP B Nodes | Delta | Functions Added | Functions Removed | Functions Modified | For each modified function: list changed steps with brief diff summary.
Compare.step3: Generate structured diff report: | Aspect | AISOP A | AISOP B | Delta |. Aspects: Pattern, version, functional node count, tools, parameters, NLU intents, function count, I2 guard coverage, weighted_score (if available from .version_history/). Highlight additions, removals, and changes. CROSS-MODULE IMPACT: for multi-file diffs, analyze cross-module data flow changes: (1) New data contracts (parameters added to cross-module calls), (2) Removed data contracts (parameters no longer passed), (3) Modified data contracts (parameter type/format changes). Present as: | Data Flow | AISOP A | AISOP B | Impact |
Compare.step4: Suggest: (1) evolve the older version, (2) modify specific differences, (3) validate either file.
Compare.constraints: Read-only. No modifications. Both files must be within workspace_dir. (D) Path guard — reject '..'.
Compare.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: compare metadata only (without full function diff) -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

Explain.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | Explain AISOP concepts with concrete JSON examples. Topics: AISOP structure, Mermaid flow, functions, constraints, Error handling, fractal patterns (A-F), quality dimensions (C/I/D), Pipeline architecture.

General.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | Explain capabilities: (1) Create — full pipeline from scratch, (2) Evolve — full pipeline with structural evolution, (3) Modify — quality improvement pipeline, (4) Validate — standalone structural check, (5) Simulate — standalone coverage analysis, (6) Compare — side-by-side version comparison, (7) Explain — concepts, (8) Discover — search existing programs, (9) Deprecate — lifecycle management, (10) Export — AIAP→SKILL.md, (11) Import — SKILL.md→AIAP, (12) Package — pack/unpack .aiap archives, (13) Convert — AISOP↔AISIP format conversion. Lead with action suggestions.

General.Error: Intent unclear -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present numbered capability list -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

DiscoverStep.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | LOCAL SCAN: workspace_dir *_aiap/→read AIAP.md frontmatter(name,version,status,summary,triggers,examples,keywords,pattern). Filter archived(show separately). IDENTITY(I11): active programs→verify identity block(program_id format,publisher,verified_on). Missing→IDENTITY_UNVERIFIED. REMOTE(PL22): registry_uri non-null→GET search?q={query}&tags={keywords}. Auth: AIAP_REGISTRY_TOKEN Bearer. 401/403 without token→degrade local+WARNING. Timeout 3s. Success→merge(local priority), mark REMOTE_UNVERIFIED. Error→local only+WARNING. Cache TTL=3600s.
DiscoverStep.step2: MATCH: compare query vs each program's (a) discovery_keywords(intersection), (b) intent_examples(semantic similarity), (c) triggers(pattern match), (d) summary(domain relevance). Compute relevance_score(0-1), rank descending. Label LOCAL/REMOTE. ENDPOINTS: parse mcp→'MCP-capable', a2a→'A2A-capable'. Query mentions 'MCP/tool/API/endpoint'→boost endpoint programs. LAYER 2 STRUCTURED SEARCH: build index from extended frontmatter. Facets: by_tags, by_capabilities, by_trust_level, by_pattern, by_security_scheme. Supplement semantic matching. Include facets summary {tags:{count}, capabilities:{count}, trust_levels:{count}}.
DiscoverStep.step3: PRESENT: numbered list with name, version, status, summary, relevance_score, match_reason. If top score > 0.8: suggest 'Consider Evolve/Modify instead of Create.' If no matches: suggest 'No matching programs. Consider Create.' For REMOTE_UNVERIFIED results, offer additional actions: (a) fetch and install locally, (b) view detailed metadata, (c) verify governance_hash before operating.
DiscoverStep.step4: FOLLOW-UP: (1) Evolve matched, (2) Modify matched, (3) Create new anyway, (4) Compare two matches, (5) Refine search.
DiscoverStep.constraints: Read-only. Scan limited to workspace_dir. (D) Path guard — reject '..'. Max 50 programs scanned. Remote query requires registry_uri parameter. Remote timeout: 3 seconds. Max remote results: 20.
DiscoverStep.Error: Scan fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list directories without parsing -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

DeprecateFlow.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve target AIAP program. Read AIAP.md status. VALIDATION: archived → reject; draft → suggest delete; deprecated → offer archive; active → proceed.
DeprecateFlow.step2: DEPRECATION (active→deprecated): collect reason. Set AIAP.md: status='deprecated', deprecated_date=today, successor=user-specified-or-null, deprecation_reason=reason. Scan workspace for dependents — if found, display WARNING.
DeprecateFlow.step3: MIGRATION GUIDE: generate migration_guide.md: deprecated program info, reason, successor mapping, data migration notes, 90-day timeline. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename).
DeprecateFlow.step4: ARCHIVE (deprecated→archived): verify 90-day window elapsed (or user force-confirms). Set status='archived'. Update governance_hash. No further Evolve/Modify allowed (D9 enforcement).
DeprecateFlow.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Backup AIAP.md as .bak. Valid transitions: active→deprecated, deprecated→archived only.
DeprecateFlow.Error: Resolution fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available programs -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

ExportSKILL.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve target AIAP program. Verify AIAP.md exists and status != 'archived'.
ExportSKILL.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='skill_export', target_path, workspace_dir. MCP ADAPTER: for MCP Server wrapper generation, delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='mcp_adapter' (available via generate.aisop.json for new programs, or direct user request 'generate MCP wrapper for X').
ExportSKILL.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Target must be valid AIAP program.
ExportSKILL.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available programs -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ImportSKILL.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve SKILL.md file path. Verify file exists and readable.
ImportSKILL.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='skill_import', target_path, workspace_dir.
ImportSKILL.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Target must be valid SKILL.md file.
ImportSKILL.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: request valid path -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

PackageFlow.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: determine pack or unpack mode from user request. Pack: resolve target AIAP program directory path. Unpack: resolve .aiap file path within workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='package', pass target_path and workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.constraints: ALWAYS delegate to advisor. (D) Path guard — target must resolve within workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available AIAP programs or .aiap files in workspace -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ConvertIntent.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve source AIAP program directory path from user request. Detect target store from context or user specification. Default target stores: AISOP source → aisip_aiap_store/, AISIP source → aisop_aiap_store/.
ConvertIntent.step2: Delegate to convert.aisop.json — pass source_path, target_store, direction (if user specified). Convert module handles: auto-direction detection, §4 topology conversion (Mermaid↔JSON flow dict), functions byte-identical copy, governance file alignment, migration manifest generation.
ConvertIntent.step3: After convert module returns, prompt user to run verification: (1) Creator validate on target program, (2) Creator simulate on target program, (3) DUAL-C1/D1/D2 consistency check against _source files, (4) Optional round-trip test.
ConvertIntent.constraints: ALWAYS delegate to convert module. Source program is READ-ONLY. (D) Path guard — paths within workspace_dir only. Bidirectional: AISOP→AISIP and AISIP→AISOP supported.
ConvertIntent.Error: Source not found → list available programs. Convert fails → report error from convert module. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

SummaryEntry.step1: [ASSERT] Called via run aisop.steps_summary from a pipeline node. | COLLECT from calling node context: (a) stage_number — current stage index (0-15), (b) stage_name — node name, (c) stage_status — execution status (COMPLETED/PASS/FAIL/SKIPPED), (d) key_outputs — core results per step (max 5 items), (e) issues_found — issue count and severity level, (f) next_stage — next node to execute. (g) execution_count — re-entry count if node is in a retry loop (default 1).
SummaryEntry.constraints: SKIP CONDITION: if pipeline_context.quiet_mode=true, skip entire steps_summary sub-graph and return immediately. Quiet mode is intended for automated batch evolution (e.g. soulbot_auto_evolution) to reduce output noise.

SummaryOutput.step2: OUTPUT summary directly to user. RETURN control to calling pipeline node — pipeline continues to next node without interruption.
SummaryOutput.constraints: Summary length MUST be 300-500 characters. Pure L1 output — no L0 JSON, no L2 audit for this sub-graph. Non-blocking — must not interrupt pipeline execution flow. PipelineStart (stage 0) summary SHOULD include pipeline overview: mode, target, version, module count. ReviewStep (stage 15) summary SHOULD be minimal: 'Pipeline 完成。详细报告见下方。' to avoid duplication with ReviewStep's own output. Gate nodes (QualityGate, PostSimulateGate) SHOULD include gate result (PASS/FAIL) and retry count if FAIL. Re-entry nodes (ModifyStep via gate FAIL loop) SHOULD include execution_count to distinguish retry passes.
